text: This exceptional strategy game stands out from the rest, offering a unique experience that seamlessly integrates with other notable titles in the genre, such as European Escalation and ALB. The sheer scale of the game is impressive, boasting an extensive array of units that adds depth and complexity to the gameplay. The visuals are stunning, with beautifully rendered graphics that immerse players in the game world. The sound design is equally impressive, complementing the on-screen action with a rich and engaging audio experience. The overall gameplay is nothing short of epic, with a steep learning curve that rewards careful planning and strategic thinking. Even the in-game chat feature, accessible from the main menu, offers a surprisingly entertaining distraction. However, newcomers to the strategy genre should be prepared for a challenging experience, as mastering the game's intricacies can take time. It's essential to approach the game with a clear head, always keeping a watchful eye on unit deployment and ensuring adequate defenses against air and tank attacks.

Last night in class, we explored this week's assigned reading: Edward S. Casey's "Getting Back into Place: Toward a Renewed Study of the Place-World." The first chapter, "Implacement," and the second, "Displacement," delved into how our modern world emphasizes time as the key framework: we are constantly racing against time, trying to save it, or catching up just in time. Casey argues that centering culture around place fosters a more rooted community. (This is a brief summary, offered humbly in case my professor or any classmates read this post.) So far, this book is the first in our course that has truly captivated my interest.

Scotland's Justice Minister has warned bigoted soccer fans that she intends to hit them "where it hurts most" by banning them from matches. Cathy Jamieson stated that exclusion orders are one of a series of measures being considered in the Scottish Executive's campaign against sectarianism. She praised Celtic and Rangers for their efforts in tackling the problem. However, the minister emphasized that stopping sectarian abuse associated with Old Firm matches is a key objective. Ms Jamieson made the comments ahead of the third-round Scottish Cup clash between the Glasgow clubs at Parkhead on Sunday. The sectarianism long associated with sections of the support from both clubs has become a significant target for the Executive. Last week, Ms Jamieson and First Minister Jack McConnell met with supporters' representatives from both clubs to discuss the issue.

To do two things at once is to do neither." It's a great critique of multitasking, isn't it? Often attributed to the Roman writer Publilius Syrus, though you know how these attributions can be—he probably never actually said it. What I'm interested in, though, is whether it's true. I mean, it's obviously true for emailing at the dinner table, texting while driving, or perhaps live-tweeting at a TED Talk. But I'd argue that for certain important activities, doing two things at once—or three, or even four—is precisely what we should aim for. Consider Albert Einstein: in 1905, he published four remarkable scientific papers.

If you can overlook the minor bugs I experienced (one annoying and the other resolved with a framerate increase from 60-90 to 80-120), I can definitely recommend this game. As most reviews focus on specs, I'll provide mine: i5 6600k (around 200€), Radeon RX480 (around 200€), Z170 Pro - Gaming (around 150€), and 16GB DDR4 RAM (around 70€). At around 650€, this rig is within the medium price range, despite being relatively new. I consistently achieved almost constant 60+ fps with vsync enabled, and I'm genuinely surprised by the 90% of comments claiming they have an overkill setup and only get 20fps.

Metal ion implantation into ceramics has emerged as a highly effective and controllable method for tailoring the surface electrical conductivity of ceramic pieces, with a wide range of applications. Notably, this approach enables the grading of voltage drops across high-voltage insulators, thereby significantly increasing the maximum operational voltage that can be applied across the insulator without surface flashover. However, a critical consideration is the long-term stability of the implantation-induced conductivity, particularly when the implanted metal species is prone to oxidation. In this study, we investigate the long-term behavior of the surface conductivity of titanium-implanted alumina, revealing that after an initial decline of approximately 40% in the first few weeks following implantation, the conductivity exhibits a remarkably slow decrease of around 10% over the subsequent year. These findings have significant implications for the development of high-reliability, high-voltage devices and systems that require precise control over the surface electrical properties of ceramic components.

*(Inhales deeply, exhales slowly)* So, I haven’t always made my living through music. For roughly five years after graduating from a respected liberal arts university, this was my daily routine. *(Chuckles)* I worked as a self-employed living statue—the "Eight-Foot Bride"—and I adore sharing this part of my past because, inevitably, people always wonder: *Who are these peculiar souls in real life?* *(Laughs softly)*  

Here’s how it went: I’d paint myself white, step onto a pedestal, place a hat or a can at my feet, and whenever someone dropped in a coin, I’d offer them a flower—along with a piercing gaze. And if they declined the flower? I’d linger with a wistful, longing gesture as they walked away.

The orientational dynamics in the isotropic phase of a comb-shaped nematic polymer featuring mesogenic and functional side groups were investigated using Kerr effect and dielectric spectroscopy. For the first time, it was observed that, unlike low-molecular-weight mesogens, the relaxation of electric birefringence in a mesogenic polymer melt above the nematic-isotropic phase transition temperature can be described by a superposition of multiple exponential processes, with two of them being dominant. These primary relaxation mechanisms alternate within a temperature range of approximately fifty degrees. Additionally, dielectric spectroscopy revealed two distinct orientational relaxation processes: the first arising from the rotation of the side mesogenic groups, and the second linked to the motion of the main chain segments.

In the fall of 1974, it felt like the thirteenth attempt to conquer the second chapter of my high school algebra book. I wasn't then, nor am I now, someone who naturally gravitates towards mathematics. In fact, I have a strong aversion to it. You get the picture: I am truly math-phobic. As I endeavored to unravel the mysteries of algebra, the doorbell unexpectedly rang. Reluctantly, I set down my book to answer the door, only to find no one there. Frustrated, I returned to my studies, and just as I began to grasp a fleeting moment of clarity, the doorbell disrupted my thoughts once more. Instantly, my fragile breakthrough vanished. Once again, I went to the door, and once again, no one was there. It was exactly what a struggling math student didn't need—an unwelcome prank.

Voters are flocking to the blog awards site as voting is underway for the annual Bloggies, which recognize the best web blogs—online spaces where people publish their thoughts—of the year. Nominations were announced on Sunday, but traffic to the official site was so heavy that it temporarily shut down due to too many visitors. Weblogs have been nominated in 30 categories, ranging from the top regional blog to the best-kept-secret blog. Blogs had a remarkable year, with a leading U.S. dictionary naming "blog" the word of 2004. Technorati, a blog search engine, tracks about six million blogs and notes that more than 12,000 are added daily. According to the U.S. research think tank Pew Internet & American Life, a blog is created every 5.8 seconds, but less than 40% of the total are updated at least once every two months.

The impact of baryonic feedback on the weak lensing angular power spectrum is a significant concern, particularly on small scales, where it induces a suppression that is not yet well understood. This knowledge gap poses a substantial challenge for forthcoming cosmological surveys, such as Euclid and LSST, which rely heavily on precise weak lensing measurements. To address this issue, we have created a simulated tomographic dataset, akin to what is expected from Euclid, for the cosmic shear power spectrum and its associated covariance matrix. Notably, these datasets have been corrected to account for baryonic effects using the baryonification method developed by Schneider et al. (2019). Furthermore, we have designed an emulator capable of rapidly predicting the suppression of power due to baryonic effects, thereby enabling us to conduct a comprehensive likelihood inference analysis. This analysis encompasses both cosmological and astrophysical parameters within the context of a standard ΛCDM cosmology, providing a robust framework for understanding the interplay between these parameters and the effects of baryonic feedback.

Voters are flocking to the blog awards website, where the annual Bloggies ceremony is underway, recognizing the best online weblogs that showcase the year's most thought-provoking and engaging content. The nominations were announced on Sunday, but the heavy traffic to the official site caused the website to temporarily shut down due to the overwhelming number of visitors. This year, weblogs have been nominated in 30 categories, ranging from the top regional blog to the best-kept-secret blog. The blogosphere has had a remarkable year, with the term "blog" being named as the Word of the Year in 2004 by a leading dictionary. According to Technorati, a prominent blog search engine, there are approximately six million blogs online, with over 12,000 new ones being added daily. Interestingly, a new blog is created every 5.8 seconds, according to research by the Pew Internet and American Life project. Fallout 3 had a superior story. The settlement system disrupts the narrative so much that managing them feels like tedious babysitting rather than enjoying the main plot. Certain keys are hardcoded and can't be remapped. The game is riddled with bugs—at one point, my Pip-Boy vanished permanently, forcing me to dig through console commands to restore it. Power armor lacks the prestige of earlier games; it's handed to you almost immediately, and fusion cores are overly abundant in shops. The story is mediocre, and while the game is more stable than Fallout 3 (which crashed constantly), it still falls short. It doesn't surpass Fallout 3 or New Vegas. Despite the hype, this installment disappoints in nearly every aspect.

As a palliative care physician, I have had the privilege of encountering numerous life-altering moments, but one incident stands out as the most embarrassing experience in my medical career. It occurred a couple of years ago, when I was consulted by a retired English professor in her 70s, who was diagnosed with pancreatic cancer. During our initial consultation, she presented with symptoms such as pain, nausea, and vomiting, which were causing her immense distress. As a physician, I had the opportunity to discuss these symptoms with her and explore potential treatment options. She specifically asked me about the potential benefits of medical marijuana, and I must admit that my knowledge on the subject was limited, as I had received minimal training on the topic during my medical education.

Could you remind me what I wrote about previously? Or perhaps it's time for another installment in the "Is This a Date?" saga. Following our awkward lunch, my confidence wavered, and I realized I neglected to share a crucial part of the story with you all. Before Pat arrived for lunch, Dustin began talking about what a genuinely nice guy Pat is and mentioned his efforts to find him a girlfriend. As I listened and nodded, it became apparent that we were more focused on finding a match for Dustin's best friend than engaging in the typical conversation one might have with a stranger over pink lemonade. Excusing myself to the bathroom, I caught my reflection in the mirror and silently pondered: Am I being set up with someone else? Is this how things unfold now?

This paper presents a millimeter-scale CMOS 64×64 single charged particle radiation detector system for external beam cancer radiotherapy. A 1×1 μm² diode measures energy deposition by a single charged particle in the depletion region, and the array design provides a large detection area of 512×512 μm². Instead of sensing the voltage drop caused by radiation, the proposed system measures the pulse width, i.e., the time it takes for the voltage to return to its baseline. This eliminates the need for power-hungry and large analog-to-digital converters. A prototype ASIC is fabricated in TSMC 65 nm LP CMOS process and consumes an average static power of 0.535 mW under a 1.2 V analog and digital power supply. The functionality of the entire system is successfully verified in a clinical 67.5 MeV proton beam setting.

I know this may seem unusual, but I believe robots can inspire us to become better humans. Growing up in Bethlehem, Pennsylvania, the birthplace of Bethlehem Steel, I had a unique upbringing. My father, an engineer, would teach me about the inner workings of things. We would collaborate on projects, such as model rockets and slot cars. Here's a photo of the go-kart we built together. That's me behind the wheel, accompanied by my sister and my best friend at the time. And one day, when I was around 10 years old, he came home, and at the dinner table, he announced that for our next project, we were going to build a robot, a robot, a machine that would learn and adapt to its environment, a true marvel of engineering and innovation.

Subject: Warning - Mailbox Size Approaching Limit

Dear User,

This automated notification serves to warn you that your mailbox is nearing its maximum storage capacity. Your current mailbox size is 77,301 KB.

Mailbox Size Limits:
Upon reaching 75,000 KB, you will receive this alert.

To check your mailbox size:
1. Right-click on the mailbox (Outlook Today).
2. Select "Properties."
3. Click the "Folder Size" button.

Note: You can use this method for individual folders as well.

To free up space, please delete any unnecessary items like sent messages and journal entries. Remember to empty the "Deleted Items" folder after removal to ensure space is actually made available.

To disable journaling:
1. Click "Tools" 
2. Go to "Options"
3. Select "Journaling Options" and deselect all items.

For further assistance, please refer to the client help section.

Thank you.

There are currently hundreds of thousands of people on transplant lists, waiting for critical organs like kidneys, hearts, and livers that could save their lives. Unfortunately, there aren’t nearly enough donor organs available to meet that demand. What if, instead of waiting, we could create brand-new, customized organs from scratch? That’s the idea behind bioprinting, a branch of regenerative medicine currently under development. We’re not yet able to print complex organs, but simpler tissues—including blood vessels and tubes responsible for nutrient and waste exchange—are already within our grasp. Bioprinting is a biological cousin of 3D printing, a technique that deposits layers of material on top of each other to construct a three-dimensional object one slice at a time.

The spreadsheet below illustrates the implicit commodity charge based on the effective date of four different rates for the same rate schedule (Schedule DR). Notably, the rate schedule in effect from August 2000 through September 2000 results in a significantly higher commodity charge, as SDG&E customers will receive a lump sum reimbursement from SDG&E during these two months, leading to lower expected electric bills. Excluding the lump sum reimbursement from the implicit energy component, the average implicit commodity charge from June 2000 through January 2001 is 6.2 cents, while the average from February 2001 through December 2001 is 7.3 cents. Overall, the average implicit commodity charge from June 2000 through December 2001 remains at 6.9 cents, which would still be the case for deliveries starting on September 1, 2000.

Magic fans beware: Wizards appears to be struggling to create a decent digital game. At its core, Magic: The Gathering is a social game meant to be enjoyed with friends (or frenemies) - laughing, trash talking, and dropping game-winning bombs. Unfortunately, Wizards of the Coast seems to have overlooked this crucial aspect of the game. If you choose to play with friends, you can't earn gold, and gold is essential. Gold unlocks everything in the game, so it's understandable that they've implemented a daily cap. However, get ready for a lengthy and tedious 1v1 grind against the computer or random opponents, whom you can't even communicate with. If you opt for the free version, it will take around 4 hours a day for several weeks to unlock the cards.

Dear Kay,

I hope this message finds you well. It has been a while since we last spoke, and I wanted to take a moment to reconnect. I am currently out of town, but I will be reviewing the matter you brought to my attention as soon as I return.

Regarding the confidentiality agreements related to Enovate, I appreciate Gregg Penman sending them over to me in November. After careful review, I have a few comments that I would like to share with you. Specifically, I would like to draw your attention to paragraphs 1 and 3 of each agreement, which contain proposed changes that warrant further discussion. These provisions are standard in confidentiality agreements, particularly when two parties are evaluating a potential transaction.

Please note that I will be in touch upon my return to discuss these matters further.

Best regards,
John

UK debut for Kevin Spacey movie: Hollywood stars Kevin Spacey and Kate Bosworth attended the British premiere of the new film, Beyond the Sea, in London's Leicester Square on Thursday. Spacey, 45, wrote, directed, and starred in the film, inspired by the life of 1950s crooner Bobby Darin. "This is my tribute to someone I think was a remarkable talent," said Spacey, who, as Darin, sings all 18 songs on the film's soundtrack. Bosworth, 21, plays Darin's wife—real-life Hollywood actress Sandra Dee. "I knew absolutely nothing about Bobby Darin before this film, but now I'm a huge fan," said Bosworth, who attended the premiere with her British boyfriend Orlando Bloom.

Who wouldn’t love wearing a fez? :D This charming puzzle-platformer features a small character in a fez, exploring a vast, branching map while collecting fragments to assemble a cube. The game is delightfully entertaining, and progressing through it is genuinely enjoyable. ^_^ However, it can get a bit frustrating if you aim to collect every cube and unlock all achievements. To find them all, you’ll need to decode various puzzles—some may take mere seconds, while others might push you to consult a guide (*raises hand*—I admit, I gave up. -_-). Speaking of which, "A Complete Guide to FEZ!!!" can be a lifesaver.

The paper presents an approach to developing an open-source neural network for classifying audio segments into different types of speech, including vocalizations produced by a child wearing a recording device, vocalizations produced by other children, adult male speech, and adult female speech. The approach involves gathering a diverse set of child-centered corpora, which is a collection of transcriptions of speech produced by children, totaling 260 hours of recordings and covering 10 languages. The goal is to develop a model that can accurately classify these different types of speech, which could be useful for language acquisition studies and early diagnosis of speech disorders.
The authors propose a deep learning model based on a convolutional neural network (CNN) architecture to classify the audio segments into different categories. They also explore the use of transfer learning to fine-tune the model on a smaller dataset of child-centered corpora, which can help improve the model's performance on unseen data. The authors evaluate the performance of their model on a test set of audio segments and show that it can accurately classify the different types of speech with high accuracy. They also demonstrate the potential of their approach for real-time speech classification, which could be useful for applications such as monitoring a child's language development or detecting speech disorders in real

The CFTC has approved new NYMEX Rule 6.40C, which allows futures commission merchants to use their own internal, proprietary systems to generate average price ("AP") calculations when multiple prices are received on an order or series of orders. The rule is effective as of October 3, 2000.
The notice informs NYMEX and COMEX members that they should be aware of the new rule and its effective date. The rule allows FCMs to use their own internal, proprietary systems to calculate APs under certain conditions specified in the rule. This may provide greater flexibility for FCMs in their operations and may allow them to more efficiently calculate APs for their customers.

Arun Shourie discusses how the current Governor of Uttar Pradesh had warned, as early as 1996, about the looming threat of a potential 'third Islamic state' emerging in the subcontinent. T.V. Rajeswar, the former head of the Intelligence Bureau and the incumbent Governor of Uttar Pradesh, had foreseen this grave danger in a series of articles published in *The Hindustan Times* back in 1996. Shourie raises a pertinent question: Does the revelation that Rajeswar—and not he or Mr. Advani—authored those warnings lend them greater credibility? Or does one dismiss the concerns, labeling Rajeswar a closet communalist for his repeated assertions, and argue that he should no longer serve as Governor of Uttar Pradesh?

The storm of melancholy has lifted. A combination of my upcoming cycle and forgetting to take my Prozac until evening left me emotionally fragile and tearful. Yet life offered sweet redemption: my boyfriend's unexpected arrival with an ice cream sundae, followed by his comforting presence after I awoke from a terrifying dream about gunfire at the news office.  

This morning brings the luxury of sleeping in—no work obligations—though I must contact KMIZ for their rate card. Later, I'll cherish time with two dear friends, Amy and Kate (with hopes of squeezing in some study time). And tomorrow holds the promise of reconnecting with someone special for our long-awaited Weight Watchers meeting.

The assembly history of the Milky Way is currently being unveiled thanks to the data provided by the Gaia mission. It is likely that the globular cluster system of our galaxy has followed a similarly intricate formation path. To constrain this formation path, we explore the link between the globular clusters and the known merging events that the Milky Way has experienced. To this end, we combined the kinematic information provided by Gaia for almost all galactic clusters with the largest sample of cluster ages available, having carefully corrected for systematic errors. To identify clusters with a common origin, we analyzed their dynamical properties, particularly in the space of integrals of motion. We find that about 40% of the clusters likely formed in situ.

Elucidating the emergence of unconventional superconductivity with elevated critical temperatures in strongly correlated two-dimensional systems remains a pivotal challenge in condensed matter physics. Recently, ultracold two-dimensional Fermi gases have gained prominence as pristine and manipulable model systems, enabling the investigation of the intricate interplay between strong correlations and reduced dimensionality. However, empirical evidence of superfluidity in these systems has long been elusive. In this study, we provide definitive proof of superfluidity in an ultracold two-dimensional Fermi gas by introducing a periodic potential and observing the absence of dissipation at velocities below a critical threshold, v$_{\rm c}$. Furthermore, our measurements of v$_{\rm c}$ as a function of interaction strength reveal a pronounced maximum in the crossover regime, where bosonic and fermionic superfluidity converge. This breakthrough establishes ultracold Fermi gases as a versatile and powerful platform for exploring the profound impact of reduced dimensionality on strongly correlated superfluids.

It sounds like you had a very vivid and confusing dream. It's possible that you were in a state of sleep paralysis, which is a condition where a person is unable to move or react during sleep. This can occur due to a variety of factors, such as sleep deprivation, stress, or certain medical conditions.

Wonder why i'm feeling so drained. Nice weather this morning. It was drizzling when i came. Although in Calcutta i hear it's been raining for the past three days.

Hands down, my all-time favorite first-person shooter. This game isn't for run-and-gun Rambo types. Recoil and ballistics are remarkably realistic, with single hits to the chest, neck, or head from rifle-caliber weapons almost always resulting in death, unless the shot merely grazes you. Rounds drop significantly over distance, and short, controlled bursts are necessary to stay on target. There's no infinite ammunition pool; if you constantly reload mid-magazine, you'll end up with a pouch full of partially used magazines. Endless sprinting isn't an option either, as prolonged running will leave you gasping for breath, severely impacting your aim until you've caught your breath. To steady your aim, rest your weapon on your elbows while prone or on a windowsill. Accuracy also suffers when standing, making it essential to adapt your shooting stance.

Inescapable is the truth  a fragment of you will forever reside within me. Even if we never shared the experience of parenthood, even if we never exchanged vows, even if our bond was never formally acknowledged... I cherished you with an all-consuming passion, and the reverberations of that love still resonate deeply within my heart. The memories of our fleeting yet unforgettable nights together, when our hearts and bodies were entwined in a profound connection, remain etched in my mind like a precious gem, polished by the salt-kissed darkness of the night air... or the gentle caress of a kiss under the rhythmic patter of rain. Whenever the feeling of a shard of glass pierces my heart, I am instantly transported back to those moments. That piece of you... The eternal, unyielding love that once dwelled within me... And still endures. For her, a love that transcends mortality.

Remember Age of Mythology? Yeah, it's been that long since there's been an RTS game of such quality. Divinity: Dragon Commander comes awfully close to being one of my personal favorite RTS games. Combine this with a huge Risk-like grand strategy mode, and it hits the spot. I’m not (read: try not to be) overly biased just because this game lets you turn into a dragon, aiding your troops by supporting, attacking, and even commanding unit production, direction, and spawning all at the same time. Honestly, I do like complicated RTS games nowadays—think Civ V and so on. However, such games tend to cram complex mechanics, charts, and resources all into one overly complicated game mode.

Here, we report the observation of thermally driven transverse vibrations in a photonic crystal waveguide, comprising two parallel nanobeams separated by a 240 nm vacuum gap. Theoretical models are developed and validated to describe the transduction of beam motion into phase and amplitude modulation of a weak optical probe propagating in a guided mode of the waveguide for probe frequencies both near and far from the dielectric band edge. This research provides a foundation for evaluating the potential detrimental effects of thermal motion on optical atomic traps near the surfaces of photonic crystal waveguides, which have been designed for near-field atom trapping. Ultimately, our long-term goal is to establish strong atom-mediated links between individual phonons of vibration and single photons propagating in the guided modes of the waveguide, thereby enabling optomechanics at the quantum level, harnessing the synergy between atoms, photons, and phonons.

I encountered no performance issues even at launch, and with all the subsequent patches, the game now runs exceptionally well. In my opinion, it’s genuinely a fantastic and enjoyable game, though the format can be slightly frustrating at times—particularly how the free-roam city repopulates with enemies after each story progression. The 'dual play' segments, where you control Batman alongside another character, were a standout highlight. I also appreciated the flexibility of using a controller for most gameplay while switching to mouse and keyboard for tank combat sections. Navigating the Batmobile through the open city—especially during APC chases—becomes incredibly satisfying once you master using the brakes to drift around corners. Once you get the hang of it, cruising through Gotham is an absolute delight!

Spontaneous conversations in real-world scenarios, particularly those captured in child-centered recordings, have proven to be some of the most difficult audio files to process. Despite these challenges, developing speech processing models that can handle a broad range of conditions is crucial for language acquisition research. Such models are essential for researchers interested in analyzing the quantity and quality of speech that children hear and produce, as well as for early diagnosis and assessing the effects of remediation. In this paper, we introduce our method for creating an open-source neural network designed to classify audio segments. These segments include vocalizations made by the child wearing the recording device, other children's vocalizations, adult male speech, and adult female speech. To achieve this, we compiled diverse child-centered corpora totaling 260 hours of recordings across 10 different languages.

Metal ion implantation into ceramics has been demonstrated to be an effective and controllable technique for tailoring the surface electrical conductivity of a ceramic piece, and this approach has been used in a number of applications. Importantly, it provides a method for grading the voltage drop across high-voltage insulators, and thereby increasing the maximum operational voltage that can be applied across the insulator without surface flashover. However, a concern for the use of the method is the long-term stability of the implantation-induced conductivity, especially if the implanted metal species is readily oxidized. Here, we report on our examination of the long-term behavior of the surface conductivity of titanium-implanted alumina. The results indicate that after an initial drop of as much as 40% in the first few weeks after implantation, the conductivity shows only a very slow decrease of about 10% over the following year.

Kevin Spacey and Kate Bosworth attended the British premiere of the new film, "Beyond the Sea", in London's Leicester Square on Thursday. The event was held in honor of the Hollywood star's tribute to the life of 1950s crooner Bobby Darin. Spacey, 45, wrote, directed, and starred in the film, inspired by the life of Bobby Darin, who sings all 18 songs on the film's soundtrack. Bosworth, 21, plays Darin's wife, Sandra Dee, a real-life Hollywood actress. "I knew absolutely nothing about Bobby Darin before this film, but now I'm a huge fan," said Bosworth, who attended the premiere with her British boyfriend, Orlando Bloom.

Khyatt (non-privileged).pst El Paso tests market for mainline expansion. El Paso Natural Gas apparently received the message when its last open season for 1.2 Bcf/d of firm transportation capacity attracted bids for 14.4 Bcf/d. The pipeline has announced an open season to test market interest in a mainline expansion and to address regulatory concerns that something needs to be done to alleviate the apparent capacity constraint at the California border. El Paso has been under significant pressure from regulators and shippers to expand due to enormous demand and skyrocketing prices in California. In a Jan. 3 letter to the pipeline, Daniel M. Adamson, Director of FERC's Office of Energy Projects, urged El Paso to consider modifying its Line 2000 project to "assist the difficult situation" now facing the California gas market.

Sscott5.nsf ahimsa (uh-him-sah) noun: the principle of non-injury to living beings. [Sanskrit ahimsa: a-, not + himsa, injury (from himsati, he injures).] "As my understanding of ahimsa matured, I became more vigilant about my thoughts and speech. The lines in the anthem: scatter her enemies and make them fall; confound their policies, frustrate their knavish tricks particularly jarred with my sentiments of ahimsa," M.K. Gandhi, The Story of My Experiments with Truth, 1927. Today is October 2, the birthday of Mohandas Karamchand Gandhi, born in 1869. A man born some two centuries ago may seem irrelevant to our age, but in reality, his life and message are still applicable to our lives today.

Britain's economic future will be the central theme of Labour's election campaign, announced Chancellor Gordon Brown. His statement followed a meeting with cabinet members at Downing Street, held just before the anticipated election announcement. Brown expressed confidence that voters would acknowledge Labour's success in fostering economic stability and growth, which they pledge to maintain. Meanwhile, the Tories have shared their strategies to address "yob culture," and the Liberal Democrats have detailed their plans to reform the council tax system. Earlier, the Archbishop of Canterbury issued a public letter to all three parties, urging them to refrain from inciting fear during the election. He appealed for a campaign focused on solutions rather than one driven by attempts to scare the public about issues like terrorism, asylum, and crime.

Roxy Music to Headline Wight Music Festival
 .
Roxy Music has been confirmed as the headliner for the Wight Music Festival, which will take place from June 10-12. The original band members - Bryan Ferry, Phil Manzanera, Andy Mackay, and Paul Thompson - will take to the stage on the Saturday night of the festival. The band is also working on new material, their first since the 1982 album Avalon.
Roxy Music was formed in 1971 by Ferry, Mackay, and Brian Eno, with Manzanera joining the following year. Their first hit was 1972's "Virginia Plain," with other hits including "Street Life," "Love Is the Drug," and the 1981 John Lennon cover, "Jealous Guy.

The developers significantly improved *Shadow Warrior (2013)*—boss battles are exhilarating, and the weapon mechanics are satisfying (though perhaps not as refined as *DOOM 2016*'s, they remain well-crafted). Enemy durability is well-balanced, provided you skillfully manage elemental effects and upgrades. The upgrade system is thoughtfully designed, avoiding unnecessary complexity while maintaining depth. Limited ammunition and elemental interactions subtly encourage varied weapon usage. The game also features numerous distinct weapons—far more meaningful than *Borderlands*' reliance on randomized loot—that remain effective throughout the experience. The soundtrack is exceptional, particularly the main theme and, unsurprisingly, Stan Bush's iconic contributions.

We developed single-shot shaped pulses for ultra-high fidelity population transfer on a 3-level quantum system in lambda configuration. To ensure high fidelity, we used the Lewis-Riesenfeld (L-R) method to derive a family of solutions leading to an exact transfer, where the solutions follow a single dynamical mode of the L-R invariant. Among this family, we identified a tracking solution with a single parameter to control simultaneously the fidelity of the transfer, the population of the excited state, and robustness. We defined a measure of the robustness of an ultra-high fidelity transfer as the minimum percentile deviation on the pulse areas at which the infidelity rises above $10^{-4}$. The robustness of our shaped pulses was found to be superior to that of Gaussian and adiabatically-optimized pulses for moderate pulse areas.

I think this assassin's creed nailed the mechanics on all fronts, from ship battles to sailing, hunting, parkour, and more. I felt a strong attachment to this character as well. The story starts out rough, but it gets much better as you play. Edward was an interesting character, and so were all the side characters. When random effects are correlated with sample design variables, the conventional approach of using individual survey weights (constructed to be inversely proportional to unit survey inclusion probabilities) to form a pseudo-likelihood no longer yields asymptotically unbiased inference. We develop a weight-exponentiated formulation for the random effects distribution that achieves unbiased inference for generating hyperparameters of the random effects. We compare our approach with frequentist methods relying on numerical integration, showing that only the Bayesian method achieves both unbiased estimation with respect to the sampling design distribution and consistency with respect to the population generating distribution. Our simulations and real data example from a business establishment survey demonstrate the utility of our approach across different modeling formulations and sampling designs. This work serves as a capstone for recent developments combining traditional survey estimation approaches with the Bayesian modeling paradigm, bridging these two rich but distinct sub-fields.

Where does all the carbon dioxide we release go? Carbon plays a natural cycle, present in all living things  in the soil, in the oceans, in the atmosphere, and in rocks deep underground. When plants and other living organisms die, much of their stored carbon is released back to the atmosphere, where it's reabsorbed by new plants and organisms. However, over millions of years, some of the carbon stored in ancient trees and sea life becomes rock, and some becomes fossil fuels  coal, oil, and natural gas. Natural events like volcanic eruptions release some of the carbon trapped in rock. Human activities, such as burning fossil fuels, also release some of that prehistoric carbon. Today, humans release approximately 60 times more carbon dioxide than all the volcanoes on the planet each year.

The world's largest and most impactful environmental and industrial undertaking is located in the heart of Canada's boreal forest, the most extensive and pristine forest on the planet. This vast forest extends across northern Canada, including Labrador, where the George River Caribou Herd resides—the largest remaining wild caribou herd in the world, with approximately 400,000 animals. Unfortunately, during my visit, I was unable to spot any caribou, though the antlers bear testament to their presence. Throughout the boreal region, we are fortunate to have an incredible abundance of wetlands. Globally, wetlands are among the most threatened ecosystems, yet they are vital for cleaning air and water, sequestering significant amounts of greenhouse gases, and providing a home to a wide array of species.

The nonlinear fluorescence emission has been extensively utilized in high-resolution optical imaging applications. We investigated the anomalous saturation effect of nitrogen vacancy defects in diamond, which is a significant issue in the field of nonlinear fluorescence microscopy. The fluorescence reduction was observed under high-power laser excitation, resulting in increased nonlinearity of the fluorescence emission and altered spatial frequency distribution of the fluorescence image. To extract the high spatial frequency information, we employed a differential excitation protocol. By modulating the excitation laser's power, we achieved a spatial resolution improvement of approximately 1.6 times, surpassing the confocal microscopy's capabilities. The simplicity of the experimental setup and data processing enables us to expect that this method can be easily adapted for enhancing the spatial resolution of sensing and biological labeling in solid-state systems, where defects are present.

A warning has been issued regarding a scam website that claims to collect money on behalf of tsunami victims, urging internet users to be cautious. The site appears convincing as it uses an outdated version of the official Disasters Emergency Committee (DEC) webpage. However, the DEC has no association with the fraudulent site and has reported it to the police. This site is merely the latest in a series of scams attempting to exploit the goodwill generated by the tsunami disaster. The website link is included in a spam email currently circulating. The email's subject line is "urgent tsunami earthquake appeal," and its content features poor grammar and bad spelling, typical of many other phishing attempts.

Downloads enter US singles chart. Digital music downloads are being included in the main US singles chart for the first time. Billboard's Hot 100 chart now incorporates data from sales of music downloads, previously only assigned to a separate download chart. Green Day's 'Boulevard of Broken Dreams' is currently number two in Billboard's pop chart, and tops its digital chart. Download sales are due to be incorporated into the UK singles chart later this year. Digital sales in the US are already used to compile Billboard's Hot Digital Sales chart. They will now be tallied with sales of physical singles and airplay information to make up its new Hot 100 chart. Its second new chart - the Pop 100 - also combines airplay, digital and physical sales, but confines its airplay information to US radio stations that play chart music.

While not perfect, this game has been highly enjoyable. The save system and the possibility of sudden death can lead to some frustration. I've since picked up a third-party mod/save tool to avoid catastrophic failures. Plus, the aiming reticule is on the small side, and the zoom is underpowered. With all that said, it's been a blast. Just one more mission, just one more supply run—or I'm running away and want to get safe before shutting the game down.

Hands down, my favorite first-person shooter of all time. The run-and-gun playstyle, which is often associated with this genre, need not apply. The recoil and ballistics are remarkably realistic. A single hit to the chest, neck, or head from a rifle-caliber weapon almost always results in death, unless it merely grazes you. Rounds drop over distance and short, controlled bursts are essential if you wish to remain on target. It's crucial to be mindful of your ammunition supply, as there is no magical ammunition pool. If you reload halfway through your magazine, you'll be left with a pouch full of half-full mags. It's crucial to pace yourself, as sprinting for an extended period will inevitably lead to a decline in your aim. To maintain accuracy, it's essential to rest your weapon on your elbows while prone or on a window sill, allowing your aim to stabilize. Your accuracy will suffer if you stand upright, as the adrenaline rush will impede your ability to focus and control your breathing.

My morning commute was a harrowing experience, thanks to a massive accident at the intersection of 14th Street and Constitution Avenue in Virginia. The resulting traffic congestion was nothing short of chaotic, but I managed to expertly navigate my vehicle from the far left lane to the far right, ultimately escaping the gridlock by diverting onto Route 50. While this ordeal may be of little consequence to those unfamiliar with the area, I'm simply relieved to have arrived at my destination without further delay. On a more pleasant note, I was surprised to discover that an email I had written to Ivan Maisel of ESPN.com had been published online. A friend, Sara, stumbled upon it while searching for me on Google, and although I had my doubts about the quality of my argument, it appears that someone deemed it worthy of publication.

I have two important questions for you. First, whose legacy do you honor? And second, what principles do you uphold? I always begin my poetry workshops by asking students these questions because poetry can sometimes appear like an antiquated art form associated with old white men, seemingly born into old age, reminiscent of Benjamin Button. I pose these questions to my students and then reveal my answers in the form of three sentences: I am the daughter of Black writers, who descended from freedom fighters that broke their chains and transformed the world. They inspire me. I repeat these words as a mantra before every poetry performance. In fact, just a moment ago, I was over there in the corner, reciting them and making faces.

El Paso's non-privileged PST El Paso tests the market for mainline expansion of natural gas, apparently got the message when its last open season for 1.2 bcf/d of firm transportation capacity attracted bids for 14.4 bcf/d. The pipeline has announced an open season to test market interest in a mainline expansion and to satisfy regulatory concerns that something needs to be done to relieve the apparent capacity constraint at the California border. El Paso has been under significant pressure from regulators and shippers to expand due to enormous demand and skyrocketing prices in California. In a January 3 letter to the pipeline, Daniel M. Adamson, director of FERC's office of energy projects, urged El Paso to consider modifying its Line 2000 project to "assist the difficult situation" now facing the California gas market.

Podcasts mark the rise of DIY radio with digital music players, which can hold up to 10,000 songs, offering a vast space to fill. With more and more iPod owners filling that space with audio content created by an eclectic assortment of producers, podcasting has emerged as a popular trend. Its strongest proponent is former MTV host and VJ (video jockey) Adam Curry. Podcasting takes its name from the Apple iPod, although you don't need an iPod to create one or listen to a podcast. Essentially, a podcast is an internet-based radio show, which podcasters create in the comfort of their own homes. They require only a microphone, a PC, and some editing software. They then upload their shows to the internet, allowing others to download and listen to them, all for free.

Notification of Enron's Control Area Reserves Service Acquisition from BPA

As per Les Rawson's update, we would like to bring to your attention a significant development regarding Grays Harbor and Willamette. Effective immediately, Enron has acquired the Control Area Reserves Service from the Bonneville Power Administration (BPA). This acquisition enables our services to sell unit-contingent energy as hourly firm, as BPA will be supplying reserves for up to 60 minutes.

In the event of a unit derate or outage at Grays Harbor Paper, please notify BPA Dispatch immediately. Subsequently, the reduction in the schedule should be implemented during the next ramp. For instance, if Grays Harbor's unit trips at 13:33, we will notify BPA and adjust the schedule to zero for the 14:00 hour. It is essential to note that BPA will only carry the schedule for a maximum of 60 minutes.

One simple vitamin can significantly reduce your risk of heart disease. Eating chocolate can reduce stress levels in students, and a new drug has been shown to prolong the lives of patients with rare diseases. Headlines like these are published daily, often presenting opposite claims from one another. There can be a disconnect between the attention-grabbing headlines and the specific, incremental results of the medical research they cover. To avoid being misled by sensationalized headlines, it's essential to assess a headline's credibility by examining the original research it reports on. We've developed a hypothetical research scenario for each of these three headlines. Keep in mind the explanation of the first example, and pause at the headline to answer the question. These are simplified scenarios, but understanding the context is crucial to making informed decisions about the validity of the claims being made.

Here is the polished text:
Two twin domes, two radically opposed design cultures. One is made of thousands of steel parts, the other of a single silk thread. One is synthetic, the other organic. One is imposed on the environment, the other creates it. One is designed for nature, the other is designed by her. Michelangelo said that when he looked at raw marble, he saw a figure struggling to be free. The chisel was Michelangelo's only tool. But living things are not chiseled. They grow. And in our smallest units of life, our cells, we carry all the information that's required for every other cell to function and to replicate. Tools also have consequences. At least since the industrial revolution, the world of design has been dominated by the rigors of manufacturing and mass production.

Jason Robinson, a former rugby league international, has targeted dual-code success for England against Australia on Saturday. As a former rugby league international, Robinson has switched codes in 2000 and now leads England against Australia at Twickenham at 2 30 GMT. England have the chance to seal their third autumn international victory after successive wins over Canada and South Africa, as well as gaining revenge for June's 51-15 hammering by the Wallabies. Meanwhile, Great Britain could end 34 years of failure against Australia with victory at Elland Road.

In our recent class discussion, we delved into the assigned reading, 'Toward a Renewed Study of the Place-World' by Edward S. Casey. The first chapter, 'Implacement,' and the second, 'Displacement,' offered a fascinating exploration of the concepts that shape our understanding of the world. Casey posits that our contemporary culture and society are dominated by the force of time, which he argues is a situating force that drives our daily lives. We are constantly racing against time, trying to make the most of the hours we have, and yet, paradoxically, we are often stuck in the present moment. Casey's central argument is that by placing place at the forefront of our cultural considerations, we can cultivate a more grounded sense of community and belonging. This book has had a profound impact on me, as it is the first reading to have truly resonated with me and sparked a deeper appreciation for the importance of place in shaping our experiences.

In this paper, we investigate the landscape of the population negative log-likelihood function of Gaussian mixture models with an arbitrary number of components. Due to nonconvexity, there exist multiple local minima that are not globally optimal, even when the mixture is well-separated. We show that all local minima share the same form of structure, which involves a non-overlapping combination of fitting multiple Gaussians to a single true component and fitting a single Gaussian to multiple true components. Our results apply to the setting where the true mixture components satisfy a certain separation condition, and are valid even when the number of components is over- or under-specified. For Gaussian mixtures with three components, we obtain sharper results in terms of the scaling with the separation between the components.

Do you ever have one of those moments when you begin to reflect on life? Like you're sitting in math class bored out of your skull, and you wonder what exactly goes on in your brain that makes you understand math? And you realize that you exist and you are in math class and you wonder why. Then you realize how amazing existing is. And suddenly it hits you—the fact that you are going off on this random train of thought is incredibly pathetic. Ever had that happen? I hate that. You know what else I hate? Block schedules. Who would ever come up with that idea? Anyone can see that sitting through a two-and-a-half-hour class in any subject is cruel and unusual punishment.

Jamieson Issues Warning to Bigots Scotland's justice minister has warned bigoted soccer fans that she intends to hit them "where it hurts most" by banning them from matches. Cathy Jamieson said exclusion orders are among a series of measures being considered in the Scottish Executive's campaign against sectarianism. She praised Celtic and Rangers for their efforts in addressing the problem. However, the minister stated that stopping sectarian abuse associated with Old Firm matches is a key objective. Ms. Jamieson was speaking ahead of the third-round Scottish Cup clash between the Glasgow clubs at Parkhead on Sunday. The sectarianism long associated with certain sections of the fanbase from both clubs has become a significant target for the Executive. Last week, Ms. Jamieson and First Minister Jack McConnell met with supporters' representatives from both clubs to discuss the issue.

Routing controllability of autonomous vehicles (AVs) has been shown to reduce the impact of selfish routing on network efficiency. However, the assumption that AVs would readily allow themselves to be controlled externally by a central agency is unrealistic. In this paper, we propose a joint routing and pricing control scheme that aims to incentivize AVs to seek centrally controlled system optimal (SO) routing by saving on tolls, while user equilibrium (UE) seeking AVs and human-driven vehicles (HVs) are subject to a congestion charge. The problem is formulated as a bi-level optimization, in which dynamic tolls are optimized in the upper level, whereas the lower level is a mixed equilibrium simulation-based dynamic traffic assignment model considering a mixed fleet of AVs and HVs.

Deep neural networks (DNNs) have long been plagued by redundancy, hindering their performance across various platforms. While weight pruning has emerged as a promising solution, conventional techniques often compromise between model accuracy and regularity, resulting in subpar inference accuracy and limited acceleration capabilities. To bridge this gap, we propose a novel paradigm shift towards pattern-based sparsity, which synergistically combines pattern and connectivity sparsity to achieve a harmonious balance between accuracy and hardware efficiency. By meticulously crafting patterns, our pruning framework consistently yields unprecedented accuracy enhancements and improved feature extraction capabilities across diverse DNN architectures and datasets. Furthermore, our pattern-aware approach seamlessly integrates pattern library extraction, selection, pruning, and weight training, thereby streamlining the optimization process. This innovative pattern-based sparsity paradigm is inherently compatible with compiler optimization, paving the way for highly efficient DNN execution on mobile platforms.

Great game! It offers tons of replayability and delivers a very fun zombie survival experience. Definitely ranks in my top five zombie games of all time.

In 2004, Prince was named the top-earning music artist, surpassing icons like Madonna and Elton John according to Rolling Stone's annual list. He amassed $56.5 million (£30.4 million) from his Musicology tour and album, outperforming Madonna, who came in second with $54.9 million (£29.5 million) from her global Re-Invention Tour. Meanwhile, Simon and Garfunkel ranked tenth, earning $24.9 million (£13.4 million) with their comeback tour. Rolling Stone highlighted Prince's triumphant return to the spotlight after a decade away from the commercial mainstream. His 2004 tour alone generated $90.3 million (£48.5 million) in ticket sales and he sold 1.9 million copies of his album "Musicology.

We present a novel mixed variable symplectic (MVS) integrator for planetary systems that resolves close encounters with unparalleled accuracy. Our method combines the stability properties of symplectic integrators with a time regularization technique that reduces the step size during encounters, allowing for more accurate long-term integrations. We utilize a high-order MVS scheme, enabling us to integrate with large time steps far from close encounters while maintaining the same level of accuracy as lower-order methods in the vicinity of close encounters. Our algorithm is able to resolve almost exact collisions (with a mutual separation of a fraction of the physical radius) while using the same time step as in weakly perturbed problems, such as the solar system. We demonstrate the long-term behavior of our algorithm on systems of six super-Earths experiencing strong scattering over 50 kyr. We compare our approach to hybrid methods, such as Mercury, and show that for an equivalent computational cost, our algorithm provides significantly better energy conservation.

When I was 14, my family was in the process of adopting my younger brothers from Ethiopia. One day, my mom posed an unexpected question: "What date should we choose for their birthdays?" I replied in disbelief, "The day they were born, of course!" To which my mom responded, "Kristen, neither of your little brothers has a birth certificate, so how do you suggest we find out their actual birthdays?" This revelation was eye-opening for me. Fast forward 20 years, and I am still deeply engaged with this issue. However, instead of solely focusing on my brothers' lack of birth certificates, I am now working on finding solutions to this problem on a global scale. But you might wonder, what is the connection between birth certificates and international development?

This game was a surprising gem, defying the often misleading rave reviews associated with certain products. In this case, the reviews accurately reflect the fun of this neat little game, which masterfully blends the classic 2D platformer with the MOBA genre, pulling it off with style. I found myself enjoying characters that I wouldn't typically appreciate in other MOBA games. Initially, I had expected this preference to hold, but I decided to give a melee tank a try anyway. The outcome was pleasantly surprising, and I ended up experimenting with more characters as they were unlocked through gameplay. The art style and interface have an oddly charming quality to them. As a result, I've adopted one of the characters as my avatar and even created a wallpaper from a card set inspired by the game.

Davies favors Gloucester future, Wales hooker Mefin Davies is likely to stay with the English side Gloucester despite reported interest from the Neath-Swansea Ospreys. BBC Wales understands the Ospreys are interested in the 32-year-old, but he would prefer to remain with Gloucester. Davies, one of the stars of Saturday's RBS Six Nations win over England, is only on a one-year contract at Kingsholm. However, he has proven his worth to the Zurich Premiership side and is likely to receive a new deal next season. The summer demise of the Celtic Warriors region left Davies in the cold and forced him to take a semi-professional contract with Neath RFC. Although he got game time with the Ospreys at the request of Wales management, he admitted before his move to Gloucester that he was angry with the way he was treated.

I have always prided myself on making anyone who frequents Casa Pescatore as comfortable as possible. I want them to feel at home, just as they would in their own residence. Yesterday, I hope I succeeded. The masses all congregated on Perry Ave with what many consider 'extreme prejudice'. At 4:30pm, my best friend Eric and his son Nunu came over with a huge pan of baked ziti. The tables were set, and I had all the food I cooked ready; the bells were ringing crazily. Before I could blink, my best friend from Florida walked through the door, and that made me smile. I missed that guy so much. The bonus was him bringing his wife along. She is expecting, and this is the closest I could get to being an uncle. I spoiled that girl with tray after tray of food and tiramisu.

Estimating the intrinsic dimension of a dataset is a crucial step in many dimensionality reduction techniques. This article introduces "intrinsic," an R package featuring advanced, state-of-the-art likelihood-based estimators for determining a dataset's intrinsic dimension. The package includes methods such as the two-nn, gride, and hidalgo models. To enhance accessibility, it provides user-friendly, high-level functions built on a robust set of efficient, low-level routines. The intrinsic package offers models in two categories: homogeneous and heterogeneous intrinsic dimension estimators. The first category includes the two-nn and gride models, with functions that support inference in both frequentist and Bayesian frameworks. In the second category is the hidalgo model, a Bayesian mixture model, which incorporates an efficient Gibbs sampler for implementation.

I am an ardent cultural consumer, my daily commute facilitated by an iPod containing a diverse array of musical genres, from Wagner and Mozart to Christina Aguilera and Josh Turner, as well as country singer Kirk Franklin. As an avid reader, I devour literature from a wide range of authors, including Ian McEwan and Stephanie Meyer, and have read the Twilight tetralogy. My home theater is my sanctuary, where I indulge in a variety of DVDs, video on demand, and television shows, particularly "Law & Order: SVU," "30 Rock," and "Judge Judy" – the people, cases, and rulings are all so compelling.

Paris promise raises Welsh hopes. Has there been a better Six Nations match than Saturday's epic in Paris? And can the Welsh revival continue all the way to a first Grand Slam since 1978? Those are the two questions occupying not just Wales supporters but rugby fans as a whole after a scintillating display in Paris. Welsh legend Mervyn Davies, a member of two of three Grand Slam-winning sides of the 1970s, hailed it as 'one of the great performances of the past three decades.' Martyn Williams, Wales' two-try scorer on the day, called it 'one of the most surreal games I have ever played in.' A crestfallen France coach, Bernard Laporte, simply observed: 'There was a French half and there was a Welsh half.'

Please be advised that the CFTC has approved new Nymex Rule 6.40C ("Average Price Calculations by Futures Commission Merchants"), which is attached and is now in effect. This new rule, consistent with a recent CFTC advisory, permits FCMS, under certain conditions specified in the rule, to use their own internal, proprietary systems to generate average price ("AP") calculations when multiple prices are received on an order or series of orders.

In 1903, the President of the United States embarked on a three-day camping trip in California's Yosemite Valley. President Theodore Roosevelt slept among a grove of towering sequoia trees, braved a snowstorm, and spent countless hours engaging in lively conversation around the campfire with his host and guide, conservationist John Muir. Roosevelt, an ardent lover of the great outdoors, was also drawn to Yosemite for a more pressing reason: the valley was in peril. Although Yosemite had been protected land since 1864, the valley was still at risk of overdevelopment in 1903, the result of a decades-old struggle to strike a balance between preservation and public use. The battle for Yosemite's future began in the midst of the 1849 gold rush, when throngs of miners flocked west in search of gold in the Sierra Nevada foothills.

Embark on a nostalgic journey with this delightful trip down memory lane, as thirty-somethings who grew up in the UK can relive the excitement of watching the ever-iconic Transformers cartoon, featuring the memorable voice acting talents of Optimus, Megatron, Bumblebee, and many more. The distinctive cartoon transformation sound effects are instantly recognizable, transporting you back to a bygone era. Graphically, the cel-shaded presentation is a masterclass in nostalgic charm, exuding a sense of retro flair that is undeniably Generation 1. Beyond its retro aesthetic, the game is an absolute blast, boasting combat mechanics that are nothing short of exceptional. The seamless integration of mid-fight transformations, expertly woven into fluid combos, sets it apart from other Cybertron games. Another notable distinction is the emphasis on melee combat, which is expertly balanced by the inclusion of ranged blasters for those who prefer a more strategic approach, as seen in encounters with formidable foes like the Seekers.

Currently, most refugees live in cities rather than in refugee camps. They represent over 60 percent of the global refugee population. With the majority residing in urban areas, there is a strong need for a paradigm shift and fresh thinking. Instead of spending money on building walls, it would be more effective to invest in programs that enable refugees to help themselves. (applause) We always have to leave behind all our possessions, but not our skills and knowledge. If allowed to live productive lives, refugees can support themselves and contribute to the development of their host country. I was born in a city called Bukavu, in South Kivu, in the Democratic Republic of Congo. I am the fifth-born in a family of 12 children.

An outstanding strategy game that truly stands out! Having experienced every major title in the wargame series, including European Escalation and AirLand Battle, I can confidently say this installment excels. The unit variety is exceptional—while I can't specify the exact count, the selection is absolutely massive. Visually stunning with superb sound design, the gameplay delivers an epic experience. Even the main menu's chat feature adds unexpected entertainment value—I'm completely serious about this. That said, newcomers or those expecting an easy challenge should be prepared: mastering this game requires significant time investment. Expect heavy losses initially—the key to success lies in meticulous planning, maintaining robust anti-air defenses, and never neglecting anti-tank measures.

This game turned out to be a delightful surprise. Often, rave reviews can be misleading, but in this case, they perfectly capture the fun of this unique little game. It blends the elements of a classic 2D platformer and a MOBA with style. I enjoyed characters in this game that I typically wouldn't in other MOBA games. I had anticipated the same disinterest with others but decided to try a melee tank anyway. I was pleasantly surprised by the experience and ended up experimenting with more characters as they became available through gameplay. The art style and interface are uniquely charming. Consequently, I now use one of the characters as my avatar and have created a wallpaper for the game from a card set.

The OSCE has been invited to monitor the US presidential election by the State Department. This will be the first time the organization has been present for a US presidential election. A baker's dozen of Congressional Democrats, including four New Yorkers, have petitioned UN Secretary-General Kofi Annan to dispatch international observers to Florida to oversee the election. I disagree with this call, as the OSCE is coming to learn from the US election process, not to oversee it.

Rosneft, a Russian state-owned oil company, has purchased Yukos' oil production unit, Yuganskneftegas, for $9.4 billion in a controversial auction. However, Yukos has filed for bankruptcy in the US in an attempt to use international business law to halt the forced sale of its assets. Yukos' lawyers claim that the auction violated US bankruptcy law and that the company and its main shareholders will go after any company that buys its assets using all and every legal means.
The acquisition of Yuganskneftegas by Rosneft is the latest move in a long-running legal battle between Yukos and the Russian government. Yukos, once one of the world's largest oil companies, was founded by Mikhail Khodorkovsky in the 1990s. However, in 2003, Khodorkovsky was arrested on charges of fraud and embezzlement, and the company was nationalized by the Russian government. Since then, Yukos has been in a state of legal limbo, with the company's assets frozen and its operations suspended.
Despite this, Yukos has continued to fight to retain control of its assets, using a complex network of shell companies and legal challenges to delay the auction of its assets. The company has

A lot has been happening with me lately. Debbie argued with me over which china I was allowed to use. I mean, come on, she brought out enough expensive china to feed an entire Baptist choir! She had so many dishes—saucers, tea pots, coffee cups, wine goblets, water glasses, and seven different types of forks and spoons, along with dinner plates, salad plates, dessert plates, soup bowls, and side bowls. I was like, "OMG!" Then she yelled at me for eating appetizers while the turkey was cooking. I assured her that I would eat, trust me! And after all that, I had to rush to the hospital with my father.

Melzer Stuns Agassi: Second seed Andre Agassi suffered a comprehensive defeat by Jurgen Melzer in the quarter-finals of the SAP Open. Agassi was often baffled by the Austrian's drop shots in San Jose, losing 6-3, 6-1. Defending champion and top seed Andy Roddick rallied to beat Sweden's Thomas Enqvist 3-6, 7-6 (8-6), 7-5. Meanwhile, unseeded Cyril Saulnier defeated fourth seed Vincent Spadea 6-2, 6-4, and Tommy Haas overcame eighth seed Max Mirnyi 6-7 (2-7), 7-6 (7-3), 6-2. Melzer has now beaten Agassi in two of their three meetings. "I had a good game plan and I executed it perfectly," he said. "It's always tough to come out and play against Andre. I didn't want him to play his game. He makes you run all over the court like a dog.

This Friday, a highly anticipated rugby match is set to take place at Headingley Stadium, as Leeds prepares to face off against Saracens on February 25, 2000. In a bid to bolster their squad, the Tykes have secured the services of Newcastle prop Ed Kalman and Tom McGee from the Borders on a loan basis, while fly-half Craig McMullen has joined the team from Narbonne. Meanwhile, Saracens have made four key changes to their lineup, with Raphael Ibanez taking the hooker position, Simon Raiwalui and Ben Russell selected in the pack, and Kevin Sorrell coming in at outside centre.

Following a successful pitch inspection on Friday, the match has been given the green light to proceed as scheduled. The Leeds team will take to the field with the following lineup: Iain Balshaw; Tom Rees, Chris Christophers, Tom Bell, Matt Doherty; Craig McMullen, Andy Dickens; Tom McGee, Rob Rawlinson, Chris Gerber; Mike Murphy, Mike Palmer (captain), Andy Morgan, Tom Parks, and Chris Popham. The replacements will be Ed Kalman, Stuart Regan, David Hyde, Jonny Rigney, Chris McMullan, Chris Rock, and Bill Vickerman.

Our Northern Natural Gas Company and Transwestern Pipeline Company will support these assets. Warframe is an entertaining game for the initial hours of play. However, after those few enjoyable hours, you'll begin to realize that the game's content is largely repetitive, with limited variety in its combat, enemies, and gameplay. The one notable aspect is that the developers are actively working on updates, which typically include significant changes to the game's systems, new Warframes, and other features. While it's a great game for casual play, I'm skeptical it will maintain your interest beyond a few months unless you're deeply invested in grinding. Fortunately, the game's free nature makes it worth trying out. If you're seeking a quick distraction, give it a shot.

I sincerely apologize, as I generally aim to support free-to-play games and indie developers, but unfortunately, I didn't enjoy this game. The description seemed perfectly suited to my interests, yet the execution left me unimpressed. Despite my love for puzzle and exploration games, I found this one entirely unintuitive. Even after searching for guidance on how to progress, I simply didn't find the gameplay enjoyable. Additionally, the game seems to lack a pause button, or if it does have one, it's completely elusive to me. Given that many sequences in the game are timed, this feels like a significant oversight. Furthermore, it seems more like a collection of cutscenes than an actual game, leaving me often twiddling my thumbs.

Metal ion implantation into ceramics has been demonstrated as an effective and controllable technique for modifying the surface electrical conductivity of ceramic pieces. This approach has been utilized in various applications. Notably, it offers a method for grading the voltage drop across high voltage insulators, thereby increasing the maximum operational voltage that can be applied without surface flashover. However, a concern regarding the method is the long-term stability of the implantation-induced conductivity, particularly if the implanted metal species is prone to oxidation. Here, we report our examination of the long-term behavior of the surface conductivity of titanium-implanted alumina. The results indicate that after an initial reduction of up to 40% within the first few weeks post-implantation, the conductivity experiences only a very gradual decrease of about 10% over the subsequent year.

Introducing PulpFiction 1.0 from Freshly Squeezed Software, the latest entrant in the realm of Atom/RDF/RSS readers! This application offers a wealth of features typical of native apps. However, as a heads-up, it's not freely available, which is a recurring topic of interest online. You can explore PulpFiction with a 15-day trial before deciding to either revert to other options or invest $25 to continue usage. One standout feature is that each post is treated as an individual item that can be easily moved, stored, labeled, or deleted—an aspect likely to delight news trackers. Additionally, PulpFiction supports OPML import and export, aligning with standard expectations.

Yukos heading back to US courts, Russian oil and gas company Yukos is due in a US court on Thursday as it continues to fight for its survival. The firm is in the process of being broken up by Russian authorities to pay a $27 billion ($14 billion) tax bill. Yukos filed for bankruptcy in the US, hoping to use international business law to halt the forced sale of its key oil production unit, Yurganskneftegas. The unit was, however, sold for $9.4 billion to state oil firm Rosneft but only after the state auction had been disrupted. Yukos lawyers now say the auction violated US bankruptcy law. The company and its main shareholders have vowed to go after any company that buys its assets, using all and every legal means.

Prince Crowned 'Top Music Earner': Prince earned more than any other pop star in 2004, surpassing artists such as Madonna and Elton John in US magazine Rolling Stone's annual list. The singer accumulated $56.5 million (£30.4 million) from concerts, album sales, and publishing, thanks to his Musicology tour and album. He kept Madonna in second place, with her earning $54.9 million (£29.5 million) from her global Re-Invention Tour. Veterans Simon and Garfunkel were in 10th place, with their comeback tour contributing to earnings of $24.9 million (£13.4 million) last year. "Prince returned to center stage after a decade in the commercial wilderness," the magazine reported. The singer's 2004 tour garnered $90.3 million (£48.5 million) in ticket sales, and he sold 1.9 million copies of his latest album, Musicology.

I've been overwhelmed with an inexplicable exhaustion for the past two days. The fatigue has been relentless, leaving me feeling utterly drained. Yesterday evening, I returned home, collapsed onto my bed, and slept through the night, only rousing when my mother called me around 8 30 pm. As I slowly came to, it was pitch dark, and I hadn't even bothered to turn on the lights, so for a few disorienting moments, it was just my mother's soothing voice in the darkness, and me struggling to regain my bearings. I couldn't help but wonder why I'm feeling so utterly exhausted... The morning was surprisingly pleasant, with a gentle drizzle outside, a welcome respite from the torrential rain that, I'm told, has been relentless in Kolkata for the past three days.

Damn fine AAA FPS shooter - that's the bottom line. It looks, sounds, and plays great - excellent FPS mechanics, a nice story, cool characters, flawless voice acting, and a badass soundtrack. The notion that attempting to accomplish multiple tasks simultaneously is a recipe for mediocrity is a sentiment often encapsulated by the phrase "to do two things at once is to do neither." This astute observation, frequently attributed to the Roman writer Publilius Syrus, serves as a scathing critique of multitasking. While the authenticity of its origin may be disputed, the phrase's underlying message is undeniably thought-provoking. However, I'd like to propose a counterargument, suggesting that, in certain contexts, engaging in multiple activities concurrently can be a hallmark of exceptional productivity. A case in point is the illustrious career of Albert Einstein, who, in 1905, made a profound impact on the scientific community with the publication of four groundbreaking papers, a testament to the potential benefits of embracing multifaceted endeavors.

Well, things can get incredibly busy here at times...but there’s always room for stress-relieving activities. I’ve discovered that I’m really not great at 8-ball or ping pong!
