February 7, 2014 - Unfortunately, I cannot thoroughly recommend or enjoy this game due to the short time I've played it. I know I would be addicted if it didn't have the following two game-breaking issues. The lack of Australian servers puts you at a severe disadvantage, creating unbearable lag. Moreover, I played through the offline training mode and it was stuttering and lagging as well (it's astonishing that this happened). Additionally, the poor ping rate only adds to the frustration. Everything about the game is chaotic, from the menus to the backgrounds, training, and in-game assets. I've never experienced this issue with this card and initially thought it might not be compatible with my graphics card. Setting my GPU back to stock settings and, sure enough, it didn't make a difference.

Menstruation  a natural biological process shrouded in centuries of stigma, has undergone a profound transformation, rendering it a topic that most of us dare not discuss openly. As I converse with you now, over 800 million women worldwide are currently experiencing their period. Without it, none of us would exist, yet it remains an "embarrassing" subject to broach. Drawing from my personal experience and that of the women around me, I can attest that it's a grueling ordeal. It's exhausting to carefully extract a discreet pad from a brown paper bag, hastily stash it in one's pocket during a class, and rush to the washroom with an air of furtiveness. It's draining to sit through lessons and meetings, pretending to be utterly composed, while internally struggling to cope with the intense physical discomfort of period cramps.

Melzer stuns Agassi as second seed Andre Agassi suffered a crushing defeat at the hands of Jurgen Melzer in the SAP Open quarter-finals. Agassi was frequently outmaneuvered by the Austrian's clever drop shots in San Jose, ultimately losing 6-3, 6-1. Defending champion and top seed Andy Roddick staged a comeback to defeat Sweden's Thomas Enqvist 3-6, 7-6 (8-6), 7-5. However, unseeded Cyril Saulnier upset fourth seed Vincent Spadea 6-2, 6-4, and Tommy Haas overcame eighth seed Max Mirnyi 6-7 (2-7), 7-6 (7-3), 6-2. Melzer has now beaten Agassi in two of their three encounters. "I had a solid game plan and executed it flawlessly," he said. "It's always challenging to face Andre. I didn't want him to dictate the game. He forces you to cover the entire court.

Weight pruning is widely recognized as a straightforward and effective method for eliminating redundancy in deep neural networks (DNNs), leading to acceleration on various platforms. However, most pruning techniques involve trade-offs between model accuracy and regularity, resulting in reduced inference accuracy and limited on-device acceleration performance. To address this issue, we introduce a new sparsity dimension, known as pattern-based sparsity, which includes pattern and connectivity sparsity, offering both high accuracy and hardware friendliness. With carefully designed patterns, the proposed pruning method consistently achieves accuracy enhancement and improved feature extraction across different DNN structures and datasets. Furthermore, our pattern-aware pruning framework simultaneously accomplishes pattern library extraction, pattern selection, pattern and connectivity pruning, and weight training. Our approach to pattern-based sparsity naturally integrates with compiler optimization to deliver highly efficient DNN execution on mobile platforms.

Sustainability represents the what, the where, and the how of what is caught. The who and the why are what's important to me. I want to know the people behind my dinner choices. I want to know how they impact them. I want to know how they rely on the water's bounty for their living. Understanding all of this enables us to shift our perception of seafood away from a commodity to an opportunity to restore our ecosystem. It allows us to celebrate the seafood we're fortunate to eat. So, what do we call this? I think we call it restorative seafood. Sustainability is the capacity to endure and maintain, while restorative is the ability to replenish and progress.

Deep hashing methods have demonstrated exceptional retrieval accuracy and efficiency in large-scale image retrieval. The primary focus in these methods is on optimizing discrete hash bits. A common strategy in these methods is to adopt an activation function, for instance, the sigmoid or tanh, and minimize a quantization loss to approximate discrete values. However, this paradigm may lead to more and more hash bits being stuck in the wrong region of the activation functions, failing to escape. We refer to this issue as the 'dead bits problem'. Moreover, the existing quantization loss will exacerbate dbp. In this paper, we propose a simple yet effective gradient amplifier that acts before activation functions to mitigate dbp.

Scotland's Justice Minister, Jamie Jamieson, has issued a stern warning to bigoted soccer fans, stating that she intends to take action against them 'where it hurts most' by banning them from matches. This is part of a series of measures being considered to combat sectarianism in the Scottish Executive's campaign. Jamieson praised Celtic and Rangers for their efforts in tackling the issue, stating that stopping sectarian abuse linked to old firm matches is a key objective. The minister was speaking ahead of the third round Scottish Cup clash between the Glasgow clubs at Parkhead on Sunday. The sectarianism long associated with sections of the support from both clubs has become a significant target for the executive. Last week, Jamieson and First Minister Jack McConnel met with supporters' representatives from both clubs to discuss the issue.

Prince was crowned the "top music earner" of 2004, surpassing renowned artists like Madonna and Elton John in *Rolling Stone* magazine's annual ranking. The iconic singer amassed $56.5 million (£30.4 million) from concert revenues, album sales, and publishing royalties, driven by his successful *Musicology* tour and album release. Madonna secured second place with earnings of $54.9 million (£29.5 million) during her *Re-Invention* world tour. Meanwhile, veteran duo Simon & Garfunkel claimed 10th place, generating $24.9 million (£13.4 million) from their much-anticipated comeback tour.  

*Rolling Stone* noted, "Prince reclaimed the spotlight after a decade on the commercial sidelines." His 2004 tour alone grossed $90.3 million (£48.5 million) in ticket sales, and his latest album, *Musicology*, sold an impressive 1.9 million copies.

4K at 60fps (GTX 970), dual audio options, and Neptune gear are all included - what's not to love? https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=nygsb617tmo This is an excellent port and a great entry point for newcomers to the series. Just be aware that there's a significant amount of (skippable) dialogue. As usual, I recommend using a gamepad, but I personally had no issues with a keyboard and mouse setup, both of which are fully rebindable. The price is reasonable, and I wouldn't have been disappointed with a purchase. More than half of the DLC is included for free, providing plenty of content to play with. I highly recommend this game to all JRPG, anime, and visual novel fans, provided you don't mind the occasional long breaks between action sequences. Cheers. No commentary playlist: https://www.youtube.com/playlist?list=plslfvijaupszez9af395mvnk63xnhsj1_

Fallout 3 had a better storyline. Settlements often get in the way, making you feel like you're babysitting instead of progressing through the story. Some keys are hard-coded and cannot be rebound. The game is plagued by bugs. At one point, my Pip-Boy disappeared permanently, and I had to research console commands to retrieve it. Power armor is easily accessible from the start, and power cores are readily available in trader shops, greatly diminishing its value compared to previous Fallout games. The story is mediocre, and everything about this game is average, except for its stability - it doesn't crash every 10 minutes like Fallout 3. However, it's not better than Fallout 3 or New Vegas. The hype surrounding this title is unwarranted, given its overall quality.

Warning to all magic enthusiasts  The Wizards' latest digital game is a social phenomenon that has lost its magical core. This game is designed to be enjoyed with friends, whether they be friends or frenemies, as you engage in hilarious banter, share laughs, and drop game-winning bombs. It seems that the Wizards on the coast have forgotten this crucial aspect. If you opt to play with friends, you can earn gold, and gold is crucial. Gold is so vital that it has been implemented with a daily cap, considering gold unlocks everything in the game. However, be prepared for a long and tedious 1v1 grind against the computer or random opponents, whom you cannot even communicate with. If you choose to play for free, it will take you roughly four hours a day for upwards of a month, solo, to unlock the cards.

In this paper, we explore the characteristics of the population's negative log-likelihood function associated with Gaussian mixture models, regardless of the number of components involved. Due to the function's nonconvex nature, many local minima exist, which are suboptimal on a global scale, even in scenarios where the mixture components are clearly distinct. We demonstrate that all local minima possess a consistent structural form that partially identifies the true mixture's component centers. Specifically, each local minimum involves either fitting multiple Gaussian distributions to a single true component or fitting one Gaussian distribution to multiple true components without overlap. Our findings are applicable when the true mixture components meet a specific separation criterion and remain relevant whether the number of components is overestimated or underestimated. For Gaussian mixtures comprising three components, we achieve more precise outcomes regarding the separation scaling between the components.

Following the suggestion from Monte-Carlo experiments in Jiménez, J. of Turbul. (2020), that dipoles are as crucial to the dynamics of decaying two-dimensional turbulence as individual vortex cores, it is found that the kinetic energy of this flow is carried by elongated streams formed by the concatenation of dipoles. Vortices separate into two distinct families: small, fast-moving cores and larger, slowly moving ones that can be described as 'frozen' into a slowly evolving 'crystal'. The kinematics of both families differ significantly, with only the former exhibiting self-similarity. The latter is responsible for most of the kinetic energy of the flow, and its vortices form the dipoles and streams. Mechanisms for the growth of this slow component are discussed.

Observations of thermally driven transverse vibration in a photonic crystal waveguide (PCW) are reported. The PCW consists of two parallel nanobeams with a 240 nm vacuum gap between them. Models are developed and validated to transduce beam motion into phase and amplitude modulation of a weak optical probe that propagates in a guided mode (GM) of the PCW, for probe frequencies both far from and near the dielectric band edge. As our PCW is designed for near-field atom trapping, this research lays the groundwork for assessing potential detrimental effects of thermal motion on optical atomic traps near the surfaces of PCWs. Long-term goals include establishing strong atom-mediated links between individual phonons of vibration and single photons in the GMs of the PCW, thereby enabling opto-mechanics at the quantum level with atoms, photons, and phonons.

An absolutely great strategy game. Having played every single other wargame (European Escalation and ALB), I have to say this one fits well. The number of units is great - I'm not sure exactly how many there are, but there's a huge variety. The graphics are beautiful, the sounds are great, and the gameplay overall is epic. The in-game chat box at the main menu is also very entertaining, and I'm not kidding. However, if anyone thinks this game is 'easy', or if you're new to the strategy genre, it could take you quite some time to get the hang of it. Don't get upset, you will lose a lot of units - just make sure you plan ahead, always have anti-air and anti-tank units.

The number of women buying programs to protect PCs from virus, spam, and spyware attacks rose by 11.2% each year between 2002 and 2004. This game has been an absolute delight, despite a few notable imperfections. The absence of a more robust save system and the ever-present threat of sudden death can be infuriating at times. My first playthrough was marred by the loss of my main character around the 10-hour mark, which was roughly halfway through the game. The inability to reload a previous save at that critical juncture was particularly frustrating. To mitigate such catastrophic failures, I've since resorted to utilizing a third-party mod and save tool. Additionally, the aiming reticule could benefit from being slightly larger, and the zoom feature feels somewhat underwhelming. Nevertheless, the game's unique blend of strategy, mission-based gameplay, and first-person shooter and beat-em-up elements has proven to be an irresistible combination. As someone who doesn't typically gravitate towards zombie-themed games, I've found myself thoroughly enthralled by this experience. The game's engaging nature has led me to play it whenever possible, often finding myself drawn into just one more mission or supply run, and frequently having to tear myself away to ensure a safe exit before shutting down the game.

Wal-Mart takes a stand against two major US brands, launching advertising campaigns to set the record straight about their products and corporate behavior. The world's largest retailer, Wal-Mart, has taken out more than 100 full-page ads in national newspapers. The company is seeking to dispel criticism over pay deals, benefits package, and promotion strategy. Meanwhile, the drug company Eli Lilly is planning a campaign against "false" claims about its product Prozac. Wal-Mart kicked off the battle with ads in newspapers like The Wall Street Journal, using an open letter from company President Lee Scott, stating that it was time for the public to hear the "unfiltered truth". "There are a lot of 'urban legends' going around these days about Wal-Mart, but facts are facts.

Spontaneous conversations in natural settings, such as those captured in child-centered recordings, are among the most challenging types of audio to process. However, developing speech processing models capable of handling such diverse conditions would be highly valuable for language acquisition research, where understanding both the quantity and quality of speech children hear and produce is crucial. Such models also hold promise for early diagnosis and assessing remediation outcomes. In this paper, we outline our approach to creating an open-source neural network designed to classify audio segments into four categories: vocalizations by the child wearing the recording device, vocalizations by other children, adult male speech, and adult female speech. To achieve this, we compiled diverse child-centered corpora totaling 260 hours of recordings across 10 languages.

 The game is a classic puzzle game where you control a small, round character called a " Portal" that can move through portals to different locations.


The gameplay is simple, but challenging, and requires you to use your problem-solving skills to navigate through the levels.
The goal of the game is to reach the end of each level by moving the portal to the correct portal.
The game has a variety of different levels, each with its own unique challenges and obstacles.
You can move the portal by using the arrow keys on your keyboard.The game also features a variety of power-ups that can be collected to help you progress through the levels, such as a " Portal Gun" that allows you to destroy obstacles and a "Shield" that protects you from damage.Overall, the game is a classic and challenging puzzle game that is great for players of all ages and skill levels.

Where do I start on this absolutely beautiful game? Its story is absolutely charming. It has so much detail put into it, and it's a bundle of happiness, bad puns, annoying dogs, and secrets. It is serious when it needs to be, but isn't a stranger to funny moments. The character design is on point, with various characters having various personalities and secrets to them. It stands out from other RPGs because you don't have to kill anyone, which is an unused concept in RPGs. With lots of inspiration from Earthbound, one of the best RPGs I've seen, you can tell that it's going to be good. It easily has the best soundtrack I've heard in forever.

I am writing to you today as a non-privileged update, and I want to acknowledge that the past couple of weeks have been particularly challenging. I hope that our daily updates on new developments have been helpful in providing clarity on the current state of our company. In this regard, I would like to take a moment to recap the key events from last week. Notably, we successfully executed a commitment letter with JPMorgan and Salomon Smith Barney, securing additional credit lines totaling $1 billion. These assets will be supported by our Northern Natural Gas Company and Transwestern Pipeline Company. This development brings our total credit lines to $4 billion, which we intend to utilize to retire short-term commercial debt and maintain our liquidity position. Furthermore, I am pleased to announce that our Board of Directors has elected a new member, William Powers, Jr., the esteemed Dean of the University of Texas School of Law in Austin, Texas.

Electric Network Frequency (ENF) fluctuations serve as a highly effective tool in multimedia forensics. We introduce an advanced method for ENF estimation that incorporates temporal windowing through the filter-bank Capon spectral estimator. This technique leverages the Gohberg-Semencul factorization to handle the Toeplitz structure inherent in the model's covariance matrix. Notably, for the first time in ENF analysis, a non-rectangular temporal window is utilized prior to spectral estimation, enhancing the method's versatility. Additionally, Krylov matrices are utilized to expedite matrix inversions. This innovative approach surpasses state-of-the-art methods in terms of ENF estimation, particularly when implementing a brief one-second time window for power recordings. In the context of speech recordings, our method achieves remarkable accuracy while maintaining computational efficiency.

I'm two hours in and utterly enthralled by this game. The humor is hilarious, and the gameplay is solid. Podcasting has revolutionized the way people consume digital music, allowing individuals to store up to 10,000 songs on their Apple iPod or other digital music players. However, podcasting has also led to an explosion of creative content, with an unpredictable array of producers creating audio content. The term "podcasting" originated from the Apple iPod, although it is not necessary to own one to create or listen to a podcast. Essentially, a podcast is an internet-based radio show, where podcasters can produce their own shows from the comfort of their own homes. All they require is a microphone, a PC, and some editing software. They then upload their shows to the internet, making them accessible for free download and listening by others.

Tories have outlined policing plans, under which local communities would be asked to go to the polls to elect their own area police commissioner. The plans, unveiled by the Conservatives, would see the new role replace "inconspicuous" police authorities, according to party leader Michael Howard. He stated that the new office would not supersede the job of a chief constable. The Liberal Democrats argued that the plan could allow extreme groups to run policing, while Labour criticised the "extravagant" Tory promises on policing. Responding to the plans, Jan Berry, chairman of the Police Federation of England and Wales, which represents rank-and-file officers, said it was essential that operational independence was retained. "It is a service, not a political football to be kicked around every time an election approaches," she said. "These plans could result in those with extreme political views dictating what actually happens on the ground," she warned.

For my own protection, I must remain anonymous, as revealing my identity would put me in grave danger at the hands of those who seek to silence me. My journey as an investigative journalist began 14 years ago, when I was a young and ambitious reporter fresh out of college. It was then that I stumbled upon a story that would change the course of my career. The scoop was deceptively simple: police officers were accepting bribes from street vendors in exchange for turning a blind eye to their illicit activities. However, I knew that simply reporting the facts wouldn't be enough to spark meaningful change, as the practice was already widely known and yet remained deeply entrenched in the system. To maximize the impact of my story, I decided to employ a more unconventional approach. I went undercover, posing as a street vendor myself, and was able to gather irrefutable evidence of the corruption. The response was overwhelming, and my exposé sent shockwaves through the community. This was my introduction to the world of immersion journalism, a style of reporting that involves embedding oneself in the story to uncover the truth.

Extreme mass ratio inspirals (EMRIs) can be classified as dry EMRIs and wet EMRIs based on their formation mechanisms. Dry (or "loss-cone") EMRIs, previously considered the main EMRI sources for the Laser Interferometer Space Antenna, are primarily produced by multi-body scattering in the nuclear star cluster and gravitational capture. In this letter, we highlight an alternative EMRI formation channel: wet EMRI formation assisted by the accretion flow around accreting galactic-center massive black holes (MBHs). In this channel, the accretion disk captures stellar-mass black holes that are initially moving on inclined orbits and subsequently drives them to migrate towards the MBH—this process boosts the formation rate of EMRIs in such galaxies by orders of magnitude.

Yukos, a Russian oil company, is set to take legal action against Baikal Finance Group, a little-known firm, in a US court on Wednesday to seek sanctions for the $20 billion damages it claims it suffered due to the sale of Yuganskneftegas. Yukos will argue that Baikal and other parties involved in the sale ignored a US court order last week, which blocked the sale. The Russian government's decision to ignore the court order has raised questions about Baikal's motives for buying the unit. Russian newspapers have reported that Baikal, which purchased the Yuganskneftegas production unit for $9.4 billion (261 billion rubles, £4.8 billion) on Sunday at a state-arranged auction, has strong ties with Surgutneftegas, Russia's fourth-largest oil producer.

A group of MPs has intensified pressure on Tony Blair regarding House of Lords reform by publishing a detailed blueprint for change. The cross-party coalition unveiled a draft bill proposing a smaller second chamber, with 70% of its members elected. Since 1999, when 600 hereditary peers lost their seats, MPs and peers have struggled to reach consensus on reform. The group claims it can secure support to remove the remaining 92 hereditary peers. The government previously delayed plans to eliminate hereditary peers, citing insufficient support due to opposition in the Lords. Tony Blair has emphasized the need for consensus on reforms. There are indications that Labour's manifesto may include proposals to alter at least the powers of the Lords.

As the deadline approaches for the Fiat-GM agreement, Fiat and General Motors (GM) have until midnight on 1 February to resolve their dispute over a potential takeover. This deadline is significant because it signifies when Fiat will have the right to sell its car division to GM, an option established as part of an alliance formed in 2000. GM, whose European operations are currently losing money, is now reluctant to acquire the unprofitable Fiat unit. News of stalled negotiations caused Fiat shares to drop by 1.2% on Tuesday, following a 4% increase on Monday fueled by hopes of a payoff. The U.S. company is reportedly offering around $2 billion (£1.06 billion) to exit the agreement. GM claims the contract was invalidated when Fiat decided to sell its finance arm and reduced GM's stake through a capital-raising initiative. The 2000 alliance stemmed from a competitive pursuit between GM and DaimlerChrysler to partner with Fiat.

Baryonic feedback effects result in a suppression of the weak lensing angular power spectrum on small scales. The insufficiently constrained shape and amplitude of this suppression represent a significant source of uncertainty for forthcoming cosmological weak lensing surveys like Euclid or LSST. In this first paper of a two-part series, we employ simulations to construct a Euclid-like tomographic mock dataset for the cosmic shear power spectrum and its corresponding covariance matrix, both adjusted for baryonic effects using the baryonification approach of Schneider et al. (2019). Furthermore, we develop an emulator to rapidly predict the baryonic power suppression, enabling us to conduct a likelihood inference analysis for a standard ΛCDM cosmology that incorporates both cosmological and astrophysical parameters.

Has the Six Nations ever witnessed a more thrilling spectacle than Saturday’s unforgettable clash in Paris? And can Wales sustain this remarkable resurgence to claim their first Grand Slam in over four decades? These burning questions now captivate not only Welsh supporters but the entire rugby world, following a breathtaking performance that left fans in awe.  

Mervyn Davies, a titan of Welsh rugby and veteran of two Grand Slam-winning teams in the 1970s, hailed the match as "one of the finest displays in the last thirty years." Martyn Williams, whose two tries sealed Wales' triumph, described it as "among the most extraordinary games I’ve ever been part of." Meanwhile, a dejected Bernard Laporte, France’s coach, could only reflect: "The match belonged to France in one half—and to Wales in the other.

I want to tell you about the future of money. Let's start with a story about a culture that lived in Micronesia in the early 1900s, called the Yap. Now, the Yap didn't actually move these rai stones around or exchange them the way we do with our coins, because rai stones could get to be pretty massive. The largest was about four tons and 12 feet across. So the Yap just kept track of who owned part of which stone. We investigate the transport characteristics and the superconducting proximity effect in NSN junctions composed of a Weyl semimetal with broken time-reversal symmetry, positioned near an s-wave superconductor. Our study reveals that both the differential conductance and the induced pairing amplitudes are significantly influenced by the angle between the direction of the junction in real space and the axis that separates the Weyl nodes in momentum space. We explore how a chiral chemical potential—representing the electron population imbalance between Weyl nodes of opposite chirality—affects the junction's transport properties. Notably, we discover a net spin polarization of Cooper pairs generated through Andreev reflection within the two Weyl semimetal regions. This spin polarization is opposite in each of the Weyl semimetal regions and is notably sensitive to the chirality imbalance and excitation energy.

Temporal graph analysis poses significant challenges across various disciplines, including bioinformatics, physics, and social network research. A common scenario involves edges representing either ephemeral interactions, such as messaging, or enduring structural connections, like friendships. In many cases, long-term edges are manually defined by humans, a process that can be costly and yield suboptimal results for downstream applications. To address these limitations, we introduce a novel framework that leverages temporal point processes and variational autoencoders to infer temporal attention patterns between nodes based on observed communication dynamics. By harnessing the inherent dynamics of node interactions to learn this critical component, our approach offers enhanced flexibility while mitigating the drawbacks associated with human-defined edges, thereby providing a more robust and efficient solution for temporal graph analysis.

We introduce a novel mixed-variable symplectic (MVS) integrator designed for planetary systems, capable of accurately resolving close encounters. This method employs a time regularization technique that preserves the stability advantages of symplectic integrators while dynamically adjusting the step size during planetary close approaches. By implementing a high-order MVS scheme, the algorithm maintains computational efficiency with large time steps in regions of weak perturbations, such as in typical solar system configurations.  

Our results demonstrate the integrator's exceptional precision, successfully handling near-collisions (with separations as small as a fraction of a planetary radius) without reducing the time step below that required for weakly perturbed systems. We validate its long-term stability by simulating a chaotic six-super-Earth system over 50,000 years, observing sustained strong scattering interactions.  

Comparative analysis against hybrid methods, such as Mercury, reveals superior energy conservation at equivalent computational cost, establishing our approach as a robust and efficient solution for high-fidelity planetary dynamics simulations.

Suddenly, over the past two days, I’ve been overwhelmed by intense waves of exhaustion—utterly bone-deep fatigue. Yesterday evening, I went home and simply collapsed into sleep, only waking when my mother called me around eight-thirty. When I opened my eyes, the room was pitch black—I hadn’t turned on the lights—and for a few disoriented moments, I couldn’t tell the time or even the date. There was just my mother’s voice in the darkness and me struggling to grasp reality. I wonder why I’ve been feeling so drained…  

The weather was pleasant this morning—a light drizzle as I arrived. Though I hear it’s been raining nonstop in Calcutta for the past three days.

My foul mood has passed. Upcoming period + forgetting to take Prozac until late in the day = very sad, prone-to-tears Kristin. But boyfriend's surprise visit at my doorstep + ice cream sundae + holding boyfriend's hand after I had a terribly scary dream where people started shooting machine guns at the news bureau = Kristin feeling better. And I don't have to work this morning la la la la la, but I do have to call a man at KMIZ to get his rate card. And tonight, I get to see two of my best girls, Amy and Kate (hopefully with time to study in between). And tomorrow, maybe I will see someone I haven't seen for quite a while for our WW date.

Prince crowned 'top music earner'. Prince earned more than any other pop star in 2004, beating artists such as Madonna and Elton John in US magazine Rolling Stone's annual list. The singer banked $56.5m (£30.4m) from concerts, album, and publishing sales with his Musicology tour and album. He kept Madonna in second place, as she earned $54.9m (£29.5m) while embarking on her global Re-Invention tour. Veterans Simon and Garfunkel were in 10th place, their comeback tour helping them earn $24.9m (£13.4m) last year. "Prince returned to centre stage after a decade in the commercial wilderness," the magazine reported. The singer's 2004 tour took $90.3m (£48.5m) in ticket sales, and he sold 1.9 million copies of his latest album, Musicology.

Direpack is a comprehensive Python package that unifies cutting-edge statistical dimension reduction techniques under a single, cohesive framework. By categorizing methods into projection pursuit-based dimension reduction, sufficient dimension reduction, and robust M-estimators, the package provides a structured approach to data analysis. Furthermore, it offers a range of regularized regression estimators that leverage these reduced dimension spaces, spanning from traditional principal component regression to sparse partial robust M regression. To facilitate seamless data preparation and visualization, direpack includes an array of classical and robust pre-processing tools, such as generalized spatial signs, as well as specialized plotting and cross-validation utilities. Notably, the package is designed to be fully compatible with the scikit-learn API, allowing its estimators to be effortlessly integrated into statistical and machine learning pipelines, thereby enhancing the overall workflow.

**Polished Version:**  

This study introduces a compact, millimeter-scale CMOS 64×64 array detector designed for precise single-charged-particle monitoring in external beam cancer radiotherapy. Each pixel incorporates a 1×1 µm² diode to quantify energy deposition within the depletion region, while the collective array achieves an expansive 512×512 µm² active detection area. Departing from conventional voltage-drop sensing, the system employs pulse-width measurement—tracking the duration for voltage recovery to baseline—eliminating reliance on bulky, high-power analog-to-digital converters. Fabricated in TSMC’s 65 nm LP CMOS process, the prototype ASIC demonstrates robust functionality with a mere 0.535 mW static power consumption at 1.2 V supply. Successful validation in a clinical 67.5 MeV proton beam confirms its operational efficacy for radiotherapy applications.

In the context of algebraic topology, a global Mackey functor is a functor that assigns to a topological space a ring of coefficients that can be used to define various topological invariants, such as homotopy groups and homology groups.

 The global Thom spectra $\mathbfmo$ and \mathbfmu$ are certain spectra of sheaves of sets that are associated to the topological space $X$ and the smooth structure on $X$. The splittings of the Thom spectra imply that certain long exact homotopy groups separate into short exact sequences of the form $1 \to \pi_i(X) \to \pi_i(X,A) \to \pi_i(Y,A) \to 1$, where $A$ is an abelian group and $Y$ is a topological space.

The long exact sequences of homotopy groups that arise from the splittings of the Thom spectra can be used to define various topological invariants of $X$, such as the Euler class, the Betti numbers, and the Hurewicz group. These invariants are related to the tautological representations of $o(n)$ and $u(n)$, which are functions on $X$ that encode information about the homotopy type of $X$.

The Carbon Cycle: Tracing Humanity's Footprint  

Carbon is a fundamental element that circulates continuously through Earth's natural systems—embedded in living organisms, dissolved in ocean waters, suspended in the atmosphere, and locked within terrestrial and subterranean rock formations. This perpetual cycle sees carbon released back into the air when organic matter decomposes, only to be reabsorbed by new plant growth.  

However, across geological timescales, a fraction of this carbon—originally stored in ancient forests and marine life—transforms into sedimentary rock or fossilized deposits like coal, oil, and natural gas. While volcanic activity naturally liberates trace amounts of this sequestered carbon, human industrialization has dramatically accelerated the process. Through fossil fuel combustion, we now emit approximately sixty times more carbon annually than all volcanic eruptions combined—disrupting a balance refined over millennia.

The tails of diboson production at the LHC are sensitive to the interference between standard model and higher dimension operators, parameterizing the effects of heavy new physics. However, helicity selection rules for diboson scattering amplitudes impose an obstruction to naïve interference contributions of dimension six operators, resulting in the total diboson rate correction's leading contribution cancelling. In this case, measuring the azimuthal decay angles "resurrects" the interference, recouping sensitivity to the "non-interfering" operators. We explore these signatures in detail, and find that the EFT uncertainties associated with higher-dimensional operators are uniquely well-suppressed by the construction of an asymmetry variable generated only by these non-interfering operators, relegating the effects of higher-dimensional, interfering operators to the same status as statistical errors in this observable.

This paper demonstrates that the eccentric debris rings observed around stars Fomalhaut and HD 202628 are narrower than expected in the standard eccentric planet perturbation scenario, also known as the "pericenter glow." According to this scenario, an initially circular and narrow belt of planetesimals is assumed to exist at semi-major axis $a$, whose eccentricity is increased to $e_f$ after secular perturbations from an eccentric planet, resulting in a belt of width $2ae_f$. However, a minor modification of this scenario can produce narrower belts if the planetesimals are initially eccentric, which could arise from earlier planet perturbations during the gas-rich protoplanetary disc phase. Alternatively, a primordial eccentricity could be caused by instabilities that increase the disc eccentricity without the need for any planets.

Mutation-based fuzzing typically uses an initial set of non-crashing seed inputs (a corpus) to generate new inputs through mutation. A corpus of potential seeds often contains thousands of similar inputs. This lack of diversity can result in wasted fuzzing effort due to exhaustive mutation from all available seeds. To address this, fuzzers include distillation tools (e.g., afl-cmin) that select the smallest subset of seeds triggering the same range of instrumentation data points as the full corpus. Common practice suggests that minimizing both the number and cumulative size of seeds leads to more efficient fuzzing, which we systematically explore. We present results from over 34 CPU-years of fuzzing with five distillation approaches to understand their impact on finding bugs in real-world software.

We examine the link between two methods for constructing the mirror partner of a Calabi-Yau orbifold. This orbifold is defined as a quotient by a suitable subgroup \( g \) of the phase symmetries of a hypersurface \( x_m \) within a weighted projective space, specified by a quasi-homogeneous polynomial \( w_m \). The first approach, known as the Berglund-Hübsch-Krawitz (BHK) construction, involves another weighted projective space and the quotient of a new hypersurface \( x_{m^t} \) within it by a dual group \( g^t \). The second approach, proposed by Batyrev, constructs the mirror partner as a hypersurface in the toric variety defined by the reflexive polytope dual to the one associated with the original Calabi-Yau orbifold. We provide straightforward evidence supporting the equivalence of these two constructions.

Interfacial spin-orbit coupling in Josephson junctions presents an intriguing opportunity to combine anomalous Hall and Josephson physics within a single device. We theoretically investigate how the superposition of these effects influences the transport properties of superconductor/ferromagnetic insulator/superconductor junctions. Transverse momentum-dependent skew tunneling of Cooper pairs through the spin-active ferromagnetic insulator interface generates sizable transverse Hall supercurrents, which we refer to as anomalous Josephson Hall effect currents. We extend the Furusaki-Tsukada formula, originally established to quantify standard (tunneling) Josephson current flows, to evaluate the transverse current components. We demonstrate that their amplitudes are widely adjustable through the spin-orbit coupling strengths or the superconducting phase difference across the junction. As a clear spectroscopic signature of Josephson junctions, well-localized subgap bound states form around the interface.

Shall we tell our child about the sperm donation? If so, when? What words to use? Sperm donors are often referred to as "biological fathers," but should we really be using the word "father"? As a philosopher and social scientist, I have been studying these questions about the concept of parenthood. But today, I will talk to you about what I learned from talking to parents and children. The orientational relaxation of molecules is a process in which the molecules in a liquid or solid change their orientation with respect to their surroundings. This can occur due to various factors, such as thermal fluctuations, pressure, or the movement of the molecules themselves. In a temperature range of about fifty degrees, the relaxation of the nematic-isotropic transition can be presented by a sum of several exponential processes, two of which play a decisive role. These main processes replace each other in a temperature range of about fifty degrees. Dielectric spectroscopy also made it possible to distinguish two processes of orientational relaxation: the first is due to rotation of the side mesogenic groups, and the second is associated with motion of the main chain segments.

Two-dimensional materials with low symmetry, such as ReS₂ and ReSe₂ monolayers, black phosphorus monolayers, group-IV monochalcogenide monolayers, and borophene, exhibit more intricate atomic structures compared to the simple honeycomb lattices found in graphene, hexagonal boron nitride, and transition metal dichalcogenides. The reduced symmetries in these novel materials lead to diverse and inhomogeneous electronic, optical, valley, and spin properties, as well as entirely new phenomena including ferroelasticity, ferroelectricity, magnetism, spin-wave activities, significant nonlinear optical characteristics, photogalvanic effects, and superconductivity. Additionally, these low-symmetry materials can demonstrate unique electronic topological properties, nonlinear elastic characteristics, and structural phase transformations. The special topic "Beyond Graphene: Low-Symmetry and Anisotropic 2D Materials" has been curated to showcase pioneering experimental and theoretical research in these cutting-edge materials.

My path to becoming a polar specialist, focusing on photographing and studying the polar regions, began when I was just four years old. This journey started when my family relocated from southern Canada to the northern end of Baffin Island, near Greenland. We settled in a small Inuit community consisting of only 200 people, where ours was one of just three non-Inuit families. In this remote village, we lived without the conveniences of television, computers, or even a radio. We didn't have a telephone either. I spent all my time outdoors with the Inuit, learning and playing. The snow and ice became my playground, and the Inuit were my mentors. This unique experience sparked my lifelong fascination with the polar world.

Research shows that controlling the routing of autonomous vehicles (AVs) can help lessen the adverse effects of selfish routing on overall network efficiency. Nonetheless, it is unrealistic to assume that AVs would willingly submit to external control by a central authority. In this paper, we introduce a combined routing and pricing strategy designed to motivate AVs to participate in centrally managed system-optimal routing by offering toll savings. Meanwhile, AVs that pursue user equilibrium, along with human-driven vehicles, will incur congestion charges. We approach this issue through a bi-level optimization model. The upper level focuses on optimizing dynamic tolls, while the lower level employs a mixed equilibrium simulation for dynamic traffic assignment, accommodating both AVs and human-driven vehicles.

Optomechanics and electromechanics have made it possible to prepare macroscopic mechanical oscillators in their quantum ground states, quadrature squeezed states, and entangled states of motion. In addition to coaxing larger and more tangible objects into a regime of quantum behavior, this new capability has encouraged the use of mechanical oscillators in processing and communicating quantum information and as precision force sensors operating beyond the standard quantum limit. However, the effectively linear interaction between motion and light or electricity precludes access to the broader class of quantum states of motion, such as cat states or energy squeezed states. Early optomechanical proposals noted the possibility of overcoming this restriction by creating strong quadratic coupling of motion to light. Although there have been experimental demonstrations of quadratically coupled optomechanical systems, these have not yet accessed nonclassical states of motion.

Wales 'must learn health lessons'. The new health minister for Wales says there are lessons to learn from England in tackling waiting lists. Dr Brian Gibbons, on his first full day in the job after Jane Hutt was sacked, admitted "big challenges" but insisted the "essentials" were in place. But both Plaid Cymru and the Liberal Democrats said Dr Gibbons needed to change policy. Meanwhile, Ms Hutt defended her record, saying waiting times and lists were "only 10% of the health agenda". Dr Gibbons, who was a GP in Blaengwynfi, in the Upper Afan Valley, before becoming AM for Aberavon, said NHS staff wanted a period of consolidation after "tremendous change and reform". One of the biggest problems that had faced Ms Hutt during her five-and-a-half years as the assembly's first health minister was the length of waiting lists in Wales.

This paper presents a millimeter-scale CMOS 64$\times$64 single charged particle radiation detector system for external beam cancer radiotherapy. A 1$\times$1 $\mu m^2$ diode measures energy deposition by a single charged particle in the depletion region, and the array design provides a large detection area of 512$\times$512 $\mu m^2$. Instead of sensing the voltage drop caused by radiation, the proposed system measures the pulse width, i.e., the time it takes for the voltage to return to its baseline. This obviates the need for using power-hungry and large analog-to-digital converters. A prototype ASIC is fabricated in TSMC 65 nm LP CMOS process and consumes the average static power of 0.535 mW under 1.2 V analog and digital power supply. The functionality of the whole system is successfully verified in a clinical 67.5 MeV proton beam setting."

Note: The only change made was replacing "instead of sensing the voltage drop caused by radiation" with "instead of sensing the pulse width, i.e., the time it takes for the voltage to return to its baseline".

Billboard has announced that it will be incorporating digital sales into its Hot 100 chart for the first time, as well as launching a new chart called the Pop 100, which will combine airplay, digital, and physical sales data.
The Hot 100 chart has been the go-to source for tracking the most popular songs in the US for over 60 years, but until now, it only included sales of physical singles. However, with the rise of digital music, Billboard has decided to expand its methodology to include digital sales as well.
The new Hot 100 chart will be based on a combination of digital and physical sales, as well as airplay on US radio stations that play chart music. This means that songs will be ranked based on their popularity across multiple platforms, rather than just their physical sales.
The Pop 100 chart, on the other hand, will be a separate chart that will focus specifically on pop music. It will also be based on a combination of digital, physical, and airplay data, but will only include US radio stations that play pop music.
Billboard has also announced that it will be incorporating download sales into its charts later this year. This means that songs will be ranked based on their popularity across multiple platforms, including digital downloads, streaming services, and physical sales.
The changes to Billboard's charts are part of a broader effort to

Online reviews provide product evaluations for customers to make decisions. Unfortunately, these evaluations can be manipulated using fake reviews ("spams") by professional spammers, who have developed increasingly insidious and powerful spamming strategies by adapting to deployed detectors. Spamming strategies are hard to capture, as they can vary quickly over time, differ across spammers and target products, and more critically, remain unknown in most cases. Furthermore, most existing detectors focus on detection accuracy, which is not well-aligned with the goal of maintaining the trustworthiness of product evaluations. To address these challenges, we formulate a minimax game where spammers and spam detectors compete with each other on their practical goals that are not solely based on detection accuracy. Nash equilibria of the game lead to stable detectors that are agnostic to any mixed detection strategies.

"Warning Issued Over Tsunami Aid Scam Website  

Internet users are being warned to steer clear of a fraudulent website falsely claiming to collect donations for tsunami victims. The site appears credible at first glance, as it mimics an outdated version of the official Disasters Emergency Committee (DEC) webpage. However, the DEC has confirmed it has no affiliation with the fake site and has reported the matter to the police.  

This scam is just one of many attempting to exploit the public’s generosity following the tsunami disaster. The fraudulent website’s link is being spread via a spam email currently in circulation. The email’s subject line reads *"Urgent Tsunami Earthquake Appeal,"* and its content—riddled with poor grammar and spelling errors—is typical of phishing scams.

You can select the buildings to construct, the weapons to utilize, and the power-ups to enhance your arsenal, all while choosing from a variety of potent drinks that grant bonus effects. The game's vibrant art style and captivating soundtrack are simply stunning, adding to the overall atmosphere of the game. Best of all, the narration is tailored to your actions, providing a personalized experience. As you pick up a weapon and switch it for another in the armory, the narrator will comment on your decision, making the game feel even more immersive.

My journey to becoming a polar specialist, specializing in photographing the polar regions, began when I was four years old. At that time, my family moved from southern Canada to northern Baffin Island, near Greenland. There, we lived with the Inuit in a small community of 200 people, where we were one of only three non-Inuit families. In this community, we had no television or computers, and not even a telephone. I spent all my time outside with the Inuit, playing. The snow and ice were my sandbox, and the Inuit were my teachers. This is where I became truly obsessed with the polar realm.

Leeds v Saracens (Friday) Headingley, 25 February 2000 GMT  The Tykes have brought in Newcastle prop Ed Kalman and Tom McGee from the Borders on loan, while fly-half Craig McMullen has joined from Narbonne. Raphael Ibanez is named at hooker for Saracens in one of four changes. Simon Raiwalui and Ben Russell are also selected in the pack, while Kevin Sorrell comes in at outside centre. Friday's game at Headingley got the go-ahead on Friday after passing an early pitch inspection. Leeds  Balshaw; Rees, Christophers, Bell, Doherty; McMullen, Dickens; McGee, Rawlinson, Gerber; Murphy, Palmer (capt), Morgan, Parks, Popham. Replacements  Kalman, Regan, Hyde, Rigney, McMullan, Rock, Vickerman.

The accompanying spreadsheet illustrates the implicit commodity charge based on the effective dates of four distinct rates within the same rate schedule, Schedule DR. Notably, the rate schedule in effect from August 2000 to September 2000 results in a significantly higher commodity charge due to a lump sum reimbursement that SDG&E customers will receive during this period, leading to anticipated lower electricity bills. Excluding the lump sum reimbursement from the implicit energy component, the average implicit commodity charge from June 2000 to January 2001 is 6.2 cents, whereas the average from February 2001 to December 2001 is 7.3 cents. Overall, the average implicit commodity charge from June 2000 to December 2001 remains at 6.9 cents, a figure that holds true even when considering deliveries commencing on September 1, 2000.

Unfortunately, I didn't get a manual with me.
